fields.description:
1. Hand Positioning:
  • Start in a plank position with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
  • Spread your fingers wide apart, placing them firmly on the ground.
  1. Finger Placement:
  • Gradually lift your palms off the ground, shifting the weight onto your fingers.
  • Focus on distributing the load evenly across your fingertips and thumbs.
  1. Body Alignment:
  • Maintain a straight line from head to heels to engage your core.
  • Keep your body in a controlled and stable position throughout the exercise.
  1. Lowering Phase:
  • Slowly bend your elbows, lowering your chest towards the ground.
  • Ensure controlled movement, maintaining stability on your fingertips.
  1. Pushing Up:
  • Press through your fingertips to straighten your arms, returning to the starting position.
  • Emphasize the engagement of your fingers and thumbs throughout the push-up.
fields.description_source:
1. Hand Positioning:
- Start in a plank position with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
- Spread your fingers wide apart, placing them firmly on the ground.
2. Finger Placement:
- Gradually lift your palms off the ground, shifting the weight onto your fingers.
- Focus on distributing the load evenly across your fingertips and thumbs.
3. Body Alignment:
- Maintain a straight line from head to heels to engage your core.
- Keep your body in a controlled and stable position throughout the exercise.
4. Lowering Phase:
- Slowly bend your elbows, lowering your chest towards the ground.
- Ensure controlled movement, maintaining stability on your fingertips.
5. Pushing Up:
- Press through your fingertips to straighten your arms, returning to the starting position.
- Emphasize the engagement of your fingers and thumbs throughout the push-up.
fields.name: Finger Pushup

fields.description:
1. Start by kneeling on the floor, keeping your knees together. Place your hands on the floor in front of you, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
  2. Make sure your body forms a straight line from head to knees. Hands should be positioned below shoulders.
  3. Lower your torso towards the ground, bending your elbows while keeping your trunk stable. Keep your knees in contact with the floor.
  4. Push through your hands to return to the starting position. Be sure to maintain contraction of chest, shoulder and arm muscles at the top of the movement.
  5. Perform the desired number of repetitions, controlling the movement and maintaining good form.
fields.description_source:
1. Start by kneeling on the floor, keeping your knees together. Place your hands on the floor in front of you, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Make sure your body forms a straight line from head to knees. Hands should be positioned below shoulders.
3. Lower your torso towards the ground, bending your elbows while keeping your trunk stable. Keep your knees in contact with the floor.
4. Push through your hands to return to the starting position. Be sure to maintain contraction of chest, shoulder and arm muscles at the top of the movement.
5. Perform the desired number of repetitions, controlling the movement and maintaining good form.
fields.name: knee push-ups

fields.description:
1. Commencez par vous agenouiller sur le sol, en gardant les genoux joints. Placez vos mains sur le sol devant vous, légèrement plus larges que la largeur des épaules.
  2. Assurez-vous que votre corps forme une ligne droite de la tête aux genoux. Les mains doivent être positionnées sous les épaules.
  3. Abaissez votre torse vers le sol en pliant les coudes tout en maintenant la stabilité du tronc. Les genoux restent en contact avec le sol.
  4. Poussez à travers les mains pour remonter à la position de départ. Assurez-vous de maintenir une contraction des muscles de la poitrine, des épaules et des bras en haut du mouvement.
  5. Effectuez le nombre souhaité de répétitions, en contrôlant le mouvement et en maintenant une bonne forme.
fields.description_source:
1. Commencez par vous agenouiller sur le sol, en gardant les genoux joints. Placez vos mains sur le sol devant vous, légèrement plus larges que la largeur des épaules.
2. Assurez-vous que votre corps forme une ligne droite de la tête aux genoux. Les mains doivent être positionnées sous les épaules.
3. Abaissez votre torse vers le sol en pliant les coudes tout en maintenant la stabilité du tronc. Les genoux restent en contact avec le sol.
4. Poussez à travers les mains pour remonter à la position de départ. Assurez-vous de maintenir une contraction des muscles de la poitrine, des épaules et des bras en haut du mouvement.
5. Effectuez le nombre souhaité de répétitions, en contrôlant le mouvement et en maintenant une bonne forme.
fields.name: Pompes sur les genoux

fields.description:
1. Lie down under a high bar or suspension bar, positioned at an appropriate height.
  2. Position yourself on your back under the bar, gripping the bar with a supinated grip (palms facing you).
  3. Adjust your position so that your body is aligned straight from head to toe. Arms should be fully extended, shoulders stabilized, and legs aligned with the rest of the body.
  4. Bend your elbows and pull your chest towards the bar, contracting your back muscles. Imagine you're trying to bring your shoulder blades together.
  5. Hold the contraction at the top of the movement for a moment to maximize muscle activation.
  6. Slowly return to the starting position, extending your elbows.
fields.description_source:
1. Lie down under a high bar or suspension bar, positioned at an appropriate height.
2. Position yourself on your back under the bar, gripping the bar with a supinated grip (palms facing you).
3. Adjust your position so that your body is aligned straight from head to toe. Arms should be fully extended, shoulders stabilized, and legs aligned with the rest of the body.
4. Bend your elbows and pull your chest towards the bar, contracting your back muscles. Imagine you're trying to bring your shoulder blades together.
5. Hold the contraction at the top of the movement for a moment to maximize muscle activation.
6. Slowly return to the starting position, extending your elbows.
fields.name: Australian pull-ups

fields.description:
1. Allongez-vous sous une barre fixe ou une barre de suspension, positionnée à une hauteur appropriée.
  2. Placez-vous sur le dos sous la barre, en saisissant la barre avec une prise en supination (paumes tournées vers vous).
  3. Ajustez votre position pour que votre corps soit aligné de manière droite des pieds à la tête. Les bras doivent être complètement étendus, les épaules stabilisées, et les jambes alignées avec le reste du corps.
  4. Fléchissez les coudes et tirez votre poitrine vers la barre, en contractant les muscles du dos. Imaginez que vous essayez de rapprocher vos omoplates l'une de l'autre.
  5. Maintenez la contraction au sommet du mouvement pendant un moment pour maximiser l'activation musculaire.
  6. Revenez lentement à la position de départ en étendant les coudes.
fields.description_source:
1. Allongez-vous sous une barre fixe ou une barre de suspension, positionnée à une hauteur appropriée.
2. Placez-vous sur le dos sous la barre, en saisissant la barre avec une prise en supination (paumes tournées vers vous).
3. Ajustez votre position pour que votre corps soit aligné de manière droite des pieds à la tête. Les bras doivent être complètement étendus, les épaules stabilisées, et les jambes alignées avec le reste du corps.
4. Fléchissez les coudes et tirez votre poitrine vers la barre, en contractant les muscles du dos. Imaginez que vous essayez de rapprocher vos omoplates l'une de l'autre.
5. Maintenez la contraction au sommet du mouvement pendant un moment pour maximiser l'activation musculaire.
6. Revenez lentement à la position de départ en étendant les coudes.
fields.name: Tractions australiennes

fields.description_source:

Keep your arms in line with your shoulders. bend and lift your elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Slowly return and repeat

fields.description_source: Hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your body. Keep your palms towards your body. Lift you hands straight up until your hands are under your chin, then lower them. Repeat the exercise.

fields.description: The single-leg hip thrust is performed by placing your upper back on a weight bench, raising one leg, and extending the hip of the other leg to achieve an isolated contraction of the glute. By working each side separately, you can fully isolate your glutes unilaterally, providing maximal training stimulus.
fields.description_source:
The single-leg hip thrust is performed by placing your upper back on a weight bench, raising one leg, and extending the hip of the other leg to achieve an isolated contraction of the glute.
By working each side separately, you can fully isolate your glutes unilaterally, providing maximal training stimulus.
fields.name: Dumbbell Single-leg Hip Thrust

fields.description: Starting position: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward, facing a wall or bench for support if needed. Bend your knees into a squat position. Place your hands on the ground in front of you, shoulder-width apart, with your fingers spread wide. Make sure your elbows are under your shoulders and your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels. Upward phase: Push with your feet and hands to lift your body off the ground. Straighten your legs and arms, keeping your body aligned. Engage your core and glutes to maintain the position. If needed, rest your knees on the wall or bench for assistance.
fields.description_source:
Starting position:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward, facing a wall or bench for support if needed.
Bend your knees into a squat position.
Place your hands on the ground in front of you, shoulder-width apart, with your fingers spread wide.
Make sure your elbows are under your shoulders and your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Upward phase:
Push with your feet and hands to lift your body off the ground.
Straighten your legs and arms, keeping your body aligned.
Engage your core and glutes to maintain the position.
If needed, rest your knees on the wall or bench for assistance.
fields.name: Frog stand

fields.description: This stretch targets the gastrocnemius (the chief muscle of the calf of the leg, which flexes the knee and foot). It is easy to perform anywhere. All you need is a wall or a chair.
fields.description_source: This stretch targets the gastrocnemius (the chief muscle of the calf of the leg, which flexes the knee and foot). It is easy to perform anywhere. All you need is a wall or a chair.

fields.description: Banded plantarflexion is a great way to bridge the gap between plantarflexion range of motion and the more strenuous calf raises in weight bearing. This can help strengthen the calf muscles, load the Achilles tendon, and improve plantarflexion range of motion.

fields.description: The double leg heel raise is important to strengthen and create control and stability around the ankle and knee, and provide balance and control for the hip and pelvis.
fields.description_source: The double leg heel raise is important to strengthen and create control and stability around the ankle and knee, and provide balance and control for the hip and pelvis.

fields.description: First get into the four-footed stance. The hands are underneath the shoulders. The fingers are fanned out wide and ensure a stable stance. It is best to rest your front body weight on your thumbs and index fingers. Now place your legs hip-width apart on the yoga mat. Your thighs should be vertically below your hips. Make sure that your weight is evenly distributed between your hands and knees. Your head is an extension of your spine and you are looking down at your mat. Your back is in a neutral position. Breathe in deeply and start with the cat. With the next exhalation, round your back vertebra by vertebra. Try to pull yourself as far as possible towards the ceiling. Pull your head towards your chest and tilt your pelvis. Now inhale deeply and move into the opposite position, cow pose. Bend your back down, pull your shoulders back slightly and lift your head as far as is comfortable for you. Your gaze is directed upwards. Breathe out consciously and switch back to the cat. With the next exhalation, switch back to cow. Repeat the exercise a few times and make sure that you are in the flow.

El movimiento que debes realizar es una abertura de los brazos alineados con los hombros mientas inclinas el cuerpo hacia delante. Intenta no realizar una extensión de hombro más allá de los 90º y mantén contraída la musculatura abdominal y los glúteos. Músculos implicados: Transverso, recto abdominal y erectores espinales

fields.description:
1. Place your feet in the stirrups and assume a high plank with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
  2. Pull your knees to your right elbow, then push them back out and to the centre.

fields.description: Place the grips as if they were a continuation of the straps. We will position our arms stretched forward and our body leaning back, completely straight. The biceps muscles will activate as you bend your elbows to bring your body towards the straps. The contraction movement will end with a slight outward movement of the hands, due to the grip we are using.

The contraction movement will end with a slight outward movement of the hands, due to the grip we are using.

fields.description: Las flexiones de brazos en TRX añaden un componente de inestabilidad que hará que tus hombros y tus pectorales trabajen de manera diferente. Puedes hacer que el ejercicio sea más o menos intenso colocando tu cuerpo más paralelo o menos al suelo. Evita que tus brazos rocen con las cuerdas porque puede que no lo notes mientras entrenas, pero podrás tener rozaduras y moratones después.
fields.description_source: Las flexiones de brazos en TRX añaden un componente de inestabilidad que hará que tus hombros y tus pectorales trabajen de manera diferente. fields.description: We'll stand facing the TRX straps and grab them with our fists facing each other, facing forward, with our arms fully extended at about shoulder-width apart. We'll keep our body straight, backward, as in the previous exercises, since we'll be lifting our body toward the TRX straps by activating our biceps. Our body will be straight, with our feet flat on the floor. This time, we'll place our arms open to the sides and at chest height. The movement we'll perform is a contraction toward our chest. To achieve this, we'll bend our elbows so that, by activating our biceps, we can pull our body toward the straps.
fields.description_source: We'll stand facing the TRX straps and grab them with our fists facing each other, facing forward, with our arms fully extended at about shoulder-width apart. We'll keep our body straight, backward, as in the previous exercises, since we'll be lifting our body toward the TRX straps by activating our biceps. Our body will be straight, with our feet flat on the floor. This time, we'll place our arms open to the sides and at chest height. The movement we'll perform is a contraction toward our chest. To achieve this, we'll bend our elbows so that, by activating our biceps, we can pull our body toward the straps.
fields.name: TRX gorilla biceps curl

fields.description: Nos colocaremos de frente a las cintas del TRX y las agarraremos con los puños de las manos enfrentados y al frente, con los brazos totalmente estirados a la altura más o menos de los hombros. El cuerpo lo mantendremos recto hacia atrás, como en los anteriores ejercicios, ya que lo que vamos a hacer es mediante la activación de los bíceps, elevar el cuerpo hacia las cintas del TRX. El cuerpo estará recto y los pies apoyados en el suelo. Los brazos en esta ocasión, los vamos a colocar abiertos hacia los lados, y a la altura del pecho. El movimiento que vamos a hacer será una contracción hacia el pecho. Para conseguirlo, lo que haremos será doblar los codos para que, mediante la activación de los bíceps atraigamos el cuerpo hacia las cintas.

fields.description: Step 1: Stand comfortably and grasp the handles of the suspension system. Step 2: Lean back resting your weight on one arm, keepng the spine neutral. Step 3: Slowly curl to pull your body weight to the up position. Supinate the arm and squeeze as you approach the end position. Step 4: Lower yourself back down and repeat.

Step 3: Slowly curl to pull your body weight to the up position. Supinate the arm and squeeze as you approach the end position. Step 4: Lower yourself back down and repeat.

fields.description: colocar las cintas cruzadas, para dejar toda la fuerza en una sola cuerda y que sea más seguro el ejercicio. Para su realización nos vamos a colocar inclinados lateralmente. El brazo con el que vamos a trabajar será el que agarre la cinta del TRX que hemos montado combinando los dos agarres. El otro brazo lo dejaremos relajado lateralmente, ya que no actuará en este momento, pues sólo entrará en acción la parte del bíceps del brazo que tenemos agarrado a la cinta. El cuerpo, al igual que anteriormente, permanecerá recto, mirando al frente y cuanto más nos inclinemos mayor será la resistencia que tengamos que vencer durante el ejercicio. Ambas piernas, en este caso, estarán juntas para solamente mantener el brazo estirado para doblarlo por el codo. Este movimiento activará el bíceps y con ello trabajaremos esta parte del brazo.

colocar las cintas cruzadas, para dejar toda la fuerza en una sola cuerda y que sea más seguro el ejercicio.

fields.description: Lie on the floor with your back resting on the floor and your legs bent, feet flat on the floor, leaving about 20 cm between your feet and your buttocks. Your hands remain flat on the floor, pressed against your ears, with your fingers pointing toward your toes. Your elbows are bent and pointing toward the ceiling. This is the starting position. Now push your hips as high as you can, raising your body off the floor, and continue pushing through your hands, extending your elbows and knees, until your back is fully arched (lumbar bridge). Your head also comes off the floor and should be level with your elbows, looking back. This is the final position. The descent should be slow and controlled. Your spine should be in a convex curve. It's not good to lift your back off the floor; it should remain straight. It should be well rounded. You'll also notice a feeling of increased oxygenation in your back. After lifting off the floor, your hips should be very high, even above the level of your head. Your arms and legs should remain straight. It seems easy, but it requires a good level of flexibility. Breathing should be deep. This posture stretches the rib cage and puts pressure on the diaphragm. If the posture is performed correctly, breathing will be perfect. Never hold your breath during the exercise.

fields.name: Back bridge

fields.description: Attach the TRX or other suspension system overhead, having the handles hanging at chest level or slightly lower. Experiment to see what height allows you to set up in the best way. Stand in front of the handles and grab them with your palms facing down. Engage your abs, squeeze your glutes, take a breath and lean forward, lifting your heels off the floor. Keep your elbows straight. Take another breath and lower yourself slowly by bending your elbows. Go down until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle (to the point where your wrists are above your elbows). Hold the position for a moment. Extend your arms by engaging your triceps and bring yourself to the starting position as you exhale.
fields.description_source:
Attach the TRX or other suspension system overhead, having the handles hanging at chest level or slightly lower. Experiment to see what height allows you to set up in the best way.
Stand in front of the handles and grab them with your palms facing down.
Engage your abs, squeeze your glutes, take a breath and lean forward, lifting your heels off the floor. Keep your elbows straight.
Take another breath and lower yourself slowly by bending your elbows.
Go down until your elbows are at a 90-degree angle (to the point where your wrists are above your elbows). Hold the position for a moment.
Extend your arms by engaging your triceps and bring yourself to the starting position as you exhale.
fields.name: TRX Tricep Extension

fields.description: POSICIÓN INICIAL: Suspenda su TRX a la altura de la rodilla. Agarre las asas con un agarre prono y coloque sus piernas juntas detrás de usted. Su posición debe verse como una posición de push up. Mantenga su cuerpo en línea recta y sus brazos completamente extendidos retrayendo los omóplatos. Contraiga el suelo pelvico y el core mientras mantiene su cuerpo recto. Mueva sus manos para colocarlas juntas. Las cuerdas del TRX deben estar justo por encima de su cabeza y que deberían tener tensión. EJECUCIÓN: Comience a bajar doblando los codos hasta que las manos toquen la frente mientras inhala. No separe los codos entre ellos. Asegúrese de sólo mover los codos cuando realice el ejercicio. Empuje su torso hacia la posición inicial apretando el resto del cuerpo mientras exhala. Asegúrese de que sus escápulas siempre están retraídas.
fields.description_source:
POSICIÓN INICIAL:
Suspenda su TRX a la altura de la rodilla. Agarre las asas con un agarre prono y coloque sus piernas juntas detrás de usted. Su posición debe verse como una posición de push up. Mantenga su cuerpo en línea recta y sus brazos completamente extendidos retrayendo los omóplatos. Contraiga el suelo pelvico y el core mientras mantiene su cuerpo recto. Mueva sus manos para colocarlas juntas. Las cuerdas del TRX deben estar justo por encima de su cabeza y que deberían tener tensión.
EJECUCIÓN:
Comience a bajar doblando los codos hasta que las manos toquen la frente mientras inhala. No separe los codos entre ellos. Asegúrese de sólo mover los codos cuando realice el ejercicio. Empuje su torso hacia la posición inicial apretando el resto del cuerpo mientras exhala. Asegúrese de que sus escápulas siempre están retraídas.
fields.name: extensiones de triceps con trx

fields.description: Start with the hands on the suspension trainer and feet on the ground. Your hands should be under your shoulders with the feet slightly in front. Lower yourself by bringing the hips between the handles and flexing the elbows. Extend the elbows to come back to the starting position and repeat.
fields.description_source:
Start with the hands on the suspension trainer and feet on the ground.
Your hands should be under your shoulders with the feet slightly in front.
Lower yourself by bringing the hips between the handles and flexing the elbows.
Extend the elbows to come back to the starting position and repeat.
fields.name: TRX dips

fields.description: Los fondos se realizan con las manos en cada uno de los agarres de nuestro arnés y despegamos los pies desde el suelo para quedarnos completamente en suspensión. Desde ahí podremos ir haciendo fondos en suspensión exactamente igual que cuando se hacen en anillas o paralelas. Sin embargo, uno de los beneficios que tienen los fondos en TRX es que aumentan la inestabilidad y nos hacen realizar un esfuerzo más intenso.
fields.description_source: Los fondos se realizan con las manos en cada uno de los agarres de nuestro arnés y despegamos los pies desde el suelo para quedarnos completamente en suspensión. Sin embargo, uno de los beneficios que tienen los fondos en TRX es que aumentan la inestabilidad y nos hacen realizar un esfuerzo más intenso.
fields.name: Fondos en TRX

fields.description: Il push-down con la corda è un esercizio molto popolare per i tricipiti. È facile da imparare e da eseguire, per cui è il preferito da tutti, dai sollevatori principianti agli esperti. Di solito viene eseguito con ripetizioni moderate o elevate, come 8-12 ripetizioni o più per serie, parte di un allenamento per la parte superiore del corpo o incentrato per le braccia.

fields.name: Flessioni dei Tricipiti al Cavo

fields.description: Sdraiatevi su una panca, il bilanciere è direttamente sopra gli occhi, le ginocchia formano un leggero angolo e i piedi sono ben saldi a terra. Impugnare la sbarra con una presa stretta (circa 20 cm). Portare il peso lentamente verso il basso fino a quando le braccia sono parallele al pavimento (gomito: angolo retto), premere poi il bilanciere verso l'alto. Quando si porta il bilanciere verso il basso, non farlo scendere sui capezzoli come nel normale esercizio di panca, ma un po' più in basso.
fields.description_source: Sdraiatevi su una panca, il bilanciere è direttamente sopra gli occhi, le ginocchia formano un leggero angolo e i piedi sono ben saldi a terra. Impugnare la sbarra con una presa stretta (circa 20 cm). Portare il peso lentamente verso il basso fino a quando le braccia sono parallele al pavimento (gomito: angolo retto), premere poi il bilanciere verso l'alto. Quando si porta il bilanciere verso il basso, non farlo scendere sui capezzoli come nel normale esercizio di panca, ma un po' più in basso.
fields.name: Panca piana con presa srtetta

fields.description: Sdraiatevi su una panca declinata, il bilanciere deve trovarsi direttamente sopra gli occhi, le ginocchia sono leggermente angolate e i piedi sono ben saldi sul pavimento. Concentratevi, respirate profondamente e afferrate la sbarra a più della larghezza delle spalle. Portatela lentamente verso il basso fino a sfiorare brevemente il petto all'altezza dei capezzoli. Spingere il bilanciere verso l'alto.

Spingere il bilanciere verso l'alto.
fields.name: Panca declinata con bilanciere

fields.description: Salite sulla macchina per i sollevamenti dei polpacci, dovreste essere in grado di spingere completamente i polpacci verso il basso. State dritti, non inarcate la schiena e non piegate le gambe. Tiratevi su il più possibile. Fate una piccola pausa di 1-2 secondi e scendete lentamente.
fields.description_source: Salite sulla macchina per i sollevamenti dei polpacci, dovreste essere in grado di spingere completamente i polpacci verso il basso. State dritti, non inarcate la schiena e non piegate le gambe. Tiratevi su il più possibile. Fate una piccola pausa di 1-2 secondi e scendete lentamente.
fields.name: Sollevamento dei polpacci in piedi

fields.description: Metter le punte dei piedi sulla base di spinta della leg press. Usate i polpacci per premere il peso flettendo i piedi/le dita dei piedi in una posizione appuntita e rilasciandoli in una posizione rilassata. Questo esercizio aumenta la massa e la forza dei muscoli Gastrocnemio e Soleo come, se non meglio, di qualsiasi altro esercizio per i polpacci.
fields.description_source:
Metter le punte dei piedi sulla base di spinta della leg press. Usate i polpacci per premere il peso flettendo i piedi/le dita dei piedi in una posizione appuntita e rilasciandoli in una posizione rilassata.
Questo esercizio aumenta la massa e la forza dei muscoli Gastrocnemio e Soleo come, se non meglio, di qualsiasi altro esercizio per i polpacci.
fields.name: Pressa per polpacci alla Leg Press

fields.description: Iniziare assumendo la posizione a quattro piedi. Le mani sono sotto le spalle. Le dita sono aperte a ventaglio e assicurano una posizione stabile. È meglio appoggiare il peso anteriore del corpo sui pollici e sugli indici. Ora posizionate le gambe alla larghezza dei fianchi sul tappetino da yoga. Le cosce devono trovarsi verticalmente sotto i fianchi. Assicuratevi che il peso sia distribuito uniformemente tra le mani e le ginocchia. La testa è un prolungamento della colonna vertebrale e lo sguardo è rivolto verso il tappetino. La schiena è in posizione neutra. Inspirate profondamente e iniziate con il gatto. Con l'espirazione successiva, arrotondate la schiena vertebra per vertebra. Cercate di tirarvi il più possibile verso il soffitto. Tirate la testa verso il petto e inclinate il bacino. Ora inspirate profondamente e passate alla posizione opposta, quella della mucca. Piegate la schiena verso il basso, tirate leggermente indietro le spalle e sollevate la testa quanto più vi è comodo. Lo sguardo è rivolto verso l'alto. Espirate consapevolmente e tornate alla posizione del gatto. Con l'espirazione successiva, tornate alla mucca. Ripetete l'esercizio alcune volte e assicuratevi di essere nel flusso
fields.description_source: Iniziare assumendo la posizione a quattro piedi. Le mani sono sotto le spalle. Le dita sono aperte a ventaglio e assicurano una posizione stabile. fields.name: Esercizio di yoga: Mucca-gatto

fields.description: Sdraiarsi sulla schiena su una panca inclinata, i piedi sono su un'estremità della panca. Chiedete a un compagno o utilizzate un altro aiuto (bilanciere, ecc.) per tenerli fissi, le mani sono dietro la testa. Da questa posizione, muovete il busto verso l'alto finché la testa o i gomiti non toccano le ginocchia. Eseguire questo movimento arrotolando la schiena.
fields.description_source: Sdraiarsi sulla schiena su una panca inclinata, i piedi sono su un'estremità della panca. Chiedete a un compagno o utilizzate un altro aiuto (bilanciere, ecc.) per tenerli fissi, le mani sono dietro la testa. Da questa posizione, muovete il busto verso l'alto finché la testa o i gomiti non toccano le ginocchia. Eseguire questo movimento arrotolando la schiena.
fields.name: Addominali su panca inclinata

fields.description: Posizionare il bilanciere in alto con una presa stretta. Abbassare l'avambraccio dietro il braccio superiore mantenendo i gomiti in alto. Estendere l'avambraccio sopra la testa. Abbassare e ripetere.
fields.description_source:
Posizionare il bilanciere in alto con una presa stretta.
Abbassare l'avambraccio dietro il braccio superiore mantenendo i gomiti in alto. Estendere l'avambraccio sopra la testa. Abbassare e ripetere.
fields.name: Estensione dei Tricipiti con Bilanciere

fields.description:
1. Sedetevi sulla macchina con i piedi ben piantati, un po' più larghi della larghezza delle spalle. Spingete i talloni e stringete i glutei. Afferrate la maniglia del cavo.
  2. Sedetevi in alto con una leggera flessione delle ginocchia. Stringete gli addominali e la schiena per mantenere un angolo perpendicolare al pavimento con il busto.
  3. Portare le spalle all'indietro e verso il basso. Stringetele tra loro mentre remate, pensando di stringere una matita tra di esse. Mentre lo fate, tirate la maniglia all'indietro verso di voi, arrivando proprio sopra l'ombelico.
  4. Fermatevi qui per un momento prima di restituire la maniglia, sempre stringendo le scapole. Una volta riportato il peso sulla pila, lasciate che le scapole si rilassino, senza tirare il busto in avanti.
  5. Ripetere il movimento

fields.name: Rematore seduto al cavo

fields.description: Impugnare due manubri, le braccia sono distese, le mani sono sul fianco, i palmi rivolti verso l'interno. Piegare le braccia e portare il peso con un movimento veloce verso l'alto. Allo stesso tempo, ruotate le braccia di 90 gradi all'inizio del movimento. Nel punto più alto, ruotate un po' i pesi verso l'esterno. Senza fare pause, riportateli giù, lentamente. Non lasciate che il corpo oscilli durante l'esercizio, tutto il lavoro è svolto dai bicipiti, che sono gli unici muscoli che devono muoversi (fate attenzione ai gomiti).

fields.description: Impugnare due bilancieri, le braccia sono distese, le mani sono sul fianco, i palmi rivolti verso l'interno. Piegare un braccio alla volta e portare il peso con un movimento veloce verso l'alto. Allo stesso tempo, ruotate le braccia di 90 gradi all'inizio del movimento. Nel punto più alto, ruotate un po' il peso verso l'esterno. Senza fare pause, riportare il braccio verso il basso, lentamente, e fare lo stesso con l'altro braccio. Non lasciate che il corpo oscilli durante l'esercizio, tutto il lavoro è svolto dai bicipiti, che sono gli unici muscoli che devono muoversi (fate attenzione ai gomiti).

fields.description: Adjust the cable machine: Set the pulley to the lowest setting possible. Choose your handles: You can use neutral grip handles (palms facing each other) or straight handles depending on your preference. Neutral grip might be easier on your wrists. Stand with proper form: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged. Maintain a slight arch in your lower back throughout the exercise. Grip the handles: Grab the handles with your chosen grip and step back a small step or two until there's slight tension on the cables. Initiate the movement: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the exercise. Imagine you're giving someone a big hug. Squeeze your chest muscles as you bring your hands together in front of your chest. Control the movement: Focus on squeezing your chest muscles rather than using your arms to pull the handles. Peak contraction: Briefly hold the squeeze at the top of the movement with your hands together at chest level. Return slowly: Slowly release the tension and return the handles back down to the starting position with your arms slightly extended but not locked out.

fields.description:
1. Regolare la macchina a cavo: Impostare la puleggia sulla posizione più bassa possibile.
  2. Scegliere le maniglie: Potete usare maniglie a presa neutra (con i palmi rivolti l'uno verso l'altro) o diritta, a seconda delle vostre preferenze. La presa neutra potrebbe essere più facile per i polsi.
  3. Mettetevi in piedi con una posizione corretta: State in piedi con i piedi alla larghezza delle spalle, le ginocchia leggermente piegate e il core impegnato. Mantenete un leggero arco nella parte bassa della schiena per tutta la durata dell'esercizio.
  4. Afferrare le maniglie: Afferrare le maniglie con la presa prescelta e indietreggiare di uno o due passi fino a quando i cavi sono in leggera tensione.
  5. Iniziare il movimento: Mantenete i gomiti leggermente piegati per tutta la durata dell'esercizio. Immaginate di abbracciare qualcuno. Stringete i muscoli del petto mentre avvicinate le mani davanti al petto.
  6. Controllare il movimento: Concentratevi sulla compressione dei muscoli del petto piuttosto che sull'uso delle braccia per tirare le maniglie.
  7. Picco di contrazione: Mantenere brevemente la contrazione al culmine del movimento con le mani unite all'altezza del petto.
  8. Ritornare lentamente: Rilasciare lentamente la tensione e riportare le maniglie alla posizione di partenza con le braccia leggermente distese ma non bloccate.

fields.description: Il movimento è molto simile a quello della panca con bilanciere, ma il peso viene portato al petto in un punto più basso. Impugnate due manubri e sdraiatevi su una panca. Tenete i pesi vicino al petto, all'altezza dei capezzoli, e spingeteli verso l'alto fino a tendere le braccia. Lasciare scendere il peso lentamente e in modo controllato.

fields.description: The dumbbell chest press is a pressing exercise similar to the barbell bench press, except that it is performed with dumbbells.
fields.description_source: The dumbbell chest press is a pressing exercise similar to the barbell bench press, except that it is performed with dumbbells.
fields.name: Dumbbell Chest Press

fields.description: On a lat pull down machine, hold the bar keeping your hands relatively close. Use an underhand grip (I.E.: the back of your hand must be facing the machine). Then pull down the bar in a straight line towards the ground. Make sure your biceps are the main drivers of the motion. You will probably feel your shoulders and lats working, but make sure your biceps are working more.
fields.description_source: On a lat pull down machine, hold the bar keeping your hands relatively close. Use an underhand grip (I.E.: the back of your hand must be facing the machine). fields.description: Na máquina de puxada de costas, segure a barra com as mãos relativamente próximas. Use uma pegada pronada (P.ex.: o dorso das suas mãos vão ficar voltados para a máquina). Então puxe a barra em uma linha reta, como se fosse em direção ao chão. Garanta que seus bíceps sejam a força motriz do movimento. Você provavelmente sentirá seus ombros e latíssimo trabalhando, mas garanta que seus bíceps sejam os músculos mais afetados.

fields.description: Set up an adjustable bench at a 45-degree angle. Lay on your stomach with your head hanging just above the edge of the bench. Grab a dumbbell in each hand and set up with a good posture – core and lats engaged and shoulders neutral. Row the dumbbells toward the top of the stomach and squeeze the back at the top of the rep. Finally, lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat until all reps are completed.
fields.description_source:
Set up an adjustable bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lay on your stomach with your head hanging just above the edge of the bench.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and set up with a good posture – core and lats engaged and shoulders neutral.
Row the dumbbells toward the top of the stomach and squeeze the back at the top of the rep.
Finally, lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat until all reps are completed.
fields.name: Incline Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row

fields.description: You should have the shoulder line in front of your wrists an perform a push-up, maintaining the shoulder in the same position

fields.description: Touch your heels to your buttocks, while remaining static or moving

fields.description:
1. Start in a plank position, with your hands directly under your shoulders and your feet hip-width apart.
  2. Engage your core to keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
  3. From this position, bend your knees slightly and jump with both feet inward, bringing them as close to your core as possible
  4. As soon as your feet touch the ground, push off forcefully to jump again and return to the starting plank position.
  5. Continue repeating this jumping motion, maintaining control and core stability throughout the exercise.
  6. Make sure to keep your breathing steady while jumping, breathing deeply and controlling your breathing.
  7. Keep your gaze fixed on the ground to maintain proper spinal alignment.
  8. Repeat the jumps for the desired length of time or the number of repetitions recommended for your workout program.
  9. To finish the exercise, rest by releasing the plank position, then stretch if necessary to loosen your muscles.
fields.description_source:
1. Start in a plank position, with your hands directly under your shoulders and your feet hip-width apart.
2. Engage your core to keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
3. From this position, bend your knees slightly and jump with both feet inward, bringing them as close to your core as possible
4. As soon as your feet touch the ground, push off forcefully to jump again and return to the starting plank position.
5. Continue repeating this jumping motion, maintaining control and core stability throughout the exercise.
6. Make sure to keep your breathing steady while jumping, breathing deeply and controlling your breathing.
7. Keep your gaze fixed on the ground to maintain proper spinal alignment.
8. Repeat the jumps for the desired length of time or the number of repetitions recommended for your workout program.
9. To finish the exercise, rest by releasing the plank position, then stretch if necessary to loosen your muscles.
fields.name: Dynamic Planche

fields.description: Pour effectuer cet exercice, allongez-vous sur le dos, bras étendus au-dessus de la tête. Ensuite, contractez vos abdominaux et soulevez votre torse du sol en tendant les bras vers le plafond pour "atteindre" le plus haut possible. Revenez lentement à la position de départ en contrôlant le mouvement. Les reach ups renforcent les muscles abdominaux et améliorent la flexibilité de la colonne vertébrale. Ils peuvent être intégrés à une variété de routines d'entraînement pour travailler la force et la stabilité du tronc.

fields.description: Seated Dumbbell Curls are an effective bicep workout that isolates the muscles by stabilizing the upper body, reducing momentum that can detract from the exercise’s effectiveness. This exercise is performed sitting down with a dumbbell in each hand, focusing on controlled movement to maximize engagement of the bicep muscles.

This exercise is performed sitting down with a dumbbell in each hand, focusing on controlled movement to maximize engagement of the bicep muscles.

fields.description: Hold the barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down) approximately shoulder-width apart. Ensure your back is straight, shoulders slightly pulled back, and arms fully extended. Curl the barbell upwards towards your chest in a controlled motion, keeping your elbows close to your body. The motion should be smooth without any swinging or momentum use. Once the barbell is at chest level, pause briefly to maximize contraction in the biceps and forearms. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with a controlled movement, fully extending your arms.
fields.description_source:
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down) approximately shoulder-width apart. Ensure your back is straight, shoulders slightly pulled back, and arms fully extended.
Curl the barbell upwards towards your chest in a controlled motion, keeping your elbows close to your body. The motion should be smooth without any swinging or momentum use.
Once the barbell is at chest level, pause briefly to maximize contraction in the biceps and forearms.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position with a controlled movement, fully extending your arms.
fields.name: Reverse Grip Barbell Curls

It is common in upper-body and chest-focused muscle-building workouts, often in combination with presses or flyes from other angles to target all portions of the chest.

fields.description: The EZ-bar skullcrusher is a popular exercise targeting the triceps muscles. The EZ-bar is used more often than a straight bar. As for the name, that’s worst-case scenario. The bar should actually come down behind the head. It is usually performed for moderate to high reps as part of an upper body or arms-focused workout. No need to chase a 1RM on this move!

fields.description: The high-cable cross tricep extension is an isolation exercise for targeting the triceps, particularly the long head. It utilizes cables and handles grasped with opposite hands, allowing for a unique pressing motion that can be effective for building tricep strength. Unlike exercises that use a straight bar or rope, this variation can be more comfortable on your wrists and elbows

fields.description: The JM press keeps your shoulders stationary, relying on the elbow flexion and extension to move the weight using your triceps strength. That means you get all the benefits of the best triceps-strengthening exercises without overtaxing that delicate shoulder joint. Classic win-win!

fields.description: Setup: Place your chest on the end of a bench, using a towel or anything soft to keep your chest protected. You want your chest to be in contact with the bench around the nipple line. Your back should be almost parallel to the floor Execution: When you’re starting the exercise, focus on pulling your elbows back until you feel maximum tightness in the lats. If you want to get some trap activation with the exercise, relax your shoulder blades when the weights are hanging down and then retract them as you start the rep.

fields.name: Helms Row

fields.description: The Meadows row is a unilateral row performed with a landmine setup, overhand grip, and staggered stance. Lean your torso forward and grip the barbell. Rest the other forearm on the forward leg. Start this movement by driving the elbow behind you while retracting the shoulder-blade. Keep the working shoulder down. Pull toward your back hip until the elbow is level with your torso.

fields.description: The plank is a bodyweight exercise. As a multi-functional movement, the plank not only targets your abdominal muscles but also the spine and hip. Plank strengthens and tightens your entire body, improves your posture and balance, reduces body fat, and can help boost your metabolism. Exercises such as the “plank pose” help strengthen the stamina of stabilizing abdominal muscles. It can also help relieve back pain associated with a weakening of the function of the stabilizing muscles of the body. Planks are a versatile exercise that targets many of the most important muscle groups in the body, so they can be applied by anyone to improve endurance and overall body strength.

fields.description: Squat is a type of bodyweight exercise. It is one of the most popular exercises for strength and muscle growth. Squat is particularly effective for focusing on the muscles of the leg and hips. Squat are an easy exercise for beginners to do. It can help strengthen leg muscles, tighten hip muscles and burn calories to lose weight. It tightens the butt and legs. Squats are very effective for firming and strengthening your legs by acting on the gluteus,hip flexors, quadriceps, hamstrings and inner thigh muscles. Also, bodyweight squats can help shape your glutes and butt.

fields.description: La sentadilla es un tipo de ejercicio de peso corporal. Es uno de los ejercicios más populares para la fuerza y el crecimiento muscular. La sentadilla es particularmente efectiva para enfocarse en los músculos de la pierna y las caderas. Las sentadillas son un ejercicio fácil de hacer para los principiantes. Puede ayudar a fortalecer los músculos de las piernas, tensar los músculos de la cadera y quemar calorías para perder peso. Aprieta los glúteos y las piernas. Las sentadillas son muy efectivas para reafirmar y fortalecer las piernas al actuar sobre los glúteos, los flexores de la cadera, los cuádriceps, los isquiotibiales y los músculos internos del muslo. Además, las sentadillas con peso corporal pueden ayudar a dar forma a los glúteos.

fields.description: Jumping jack are a plyometric exercise. Plyometrics are explosive aerobic moves that increase speed, quickness, and power and they work your whole body. Jumping jack target the arm, shoulder, abdominal muscles, hip muscles and hip flexors and also work on the thighs, knee tendons and quadriceps. Jumps are beneficial to your health because they combine cardiovascular conditioning with strength work. Since jumps elevate your heart rate, they can also improve your cardiovascular fitness.

fields.description: Los saltos de tijera son un ejercicio pliométrico. Los pliométricos son movimientos aeróbicos explosivos que aumentan la velocidad, la rapidez y la potencia y trabajan todo el cuerpo. El salto de tijera se enfoca en el brazo, el hombro, los músculos abdominales, los músculos de la cadera y los flexores de la cadera y también trabaja en los muslos, los tendones de la rodilla y los cuádriceps. Los saltos son beneficiosos para la salud porque combinan el acondicionamiento cardiovascular con el trabajo de fuerza. Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.

Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.
fields.description_source:
Puedes utilizar este ejercicio tanto como calentamiento dinámico antes de entrenar como añadirlo a tu rutina de entrenamiento cardiovascular para quemar grasa.
Los saltos altos de rodilla son un ejercicio pliométrico. Los pliométricos son movimientos aeróbicos explosivos que aumentan la velocidad, la rapidez y la potencia y trabajan todo el cuerpo.
Los saltos altos de rodilla se dirigen a los oblicuos, los músculos de las piernas, los músculos de la cadera y los flexores de la cadera y también trabajan en los muslos, los tendones de la rodilla, los cuádriceps y los hombros.
Los saltos son beneficiosos para la salud porque combinan el acondicionamiento cardiovascular con el trabajo de fuerza. Dado que los saltos elevan el ritmo cardíaco, también pueden mejorar la condición cardiovascular.
fields.name: Saltos Altos De Rodilla HD

fields.description: Crunch movement is one of the most basic exercises designed to strengthen the core muscles of the body. Exercise helps to strengthen core muscles, improve posture, and increase muscle mobility and flexibility. Improves six pack muscles: When crunch exercise is done, the rectus abdominus and oblique muscles are tightened, so the upper abdominal muscles and six pack muscles develop. Increases the strength of the abdominal muscles: The primary role of your abdominal muscles is to stabilize your mid-section. It supports you while lifting heavy objects, allowing you to twist and rotate your body. These are all day long actions that you do not notice, so it is important that your abdominal muscles can sustain long hours of work. Crunch exercise helps build this important endurance in the abdominal muscles. Muscular endurance is the ability of these fibers to resist resistance for a long time.

fields.description: El movimiento crunch es uno de los ejercicios más básicos diseñados para fortalecer los músculos centrales del cuerpo. El ejercicio ayuda a fortalecer los músculos centrales, mejorar la postura y aumentar la movilidad y flexibilidad muscular. Mejora los músculos del six pack: Cuando se realiza el ejercicio de abdominales, los músculos rectos abdominales y oblicuos se tensan, por lo que se desarrollan los músculos abdominales superiores y los músculos del six pack. Aumenta la fuerza de los músculos abdominales: La función principal de los músculos abdominales es estabilizar la sección media. Te apoya mientras levantas objetos pesados, lo que te permite girar y rotar tu cuerpo. Estas son acciones de todo el día que no notas, por lo que es importante que tus músculos abdominales puedan soportar largas horas de trabajo. El ejercicio crunch ayuda a desarrollar esta importante resistencia en los músculos abdominales. La resistencia muscular es la capacidad de estas fibras para resistir la resistencia durante mucho tiempo.

fields.description: Triceps dips on floor are a compound exercise as they worked multiple muscle groups simultaneously. Although this bodyweight exercise mainly targets the triceps, it also hits your chest and front of your shoulder. Triceps dips on floor are one of the most effective exercises to increase arm strength and also build lean muscle in your upper arms. Triceps dips on floor are a closed kinetic chain exercise and express that you do the movements around a fixed point. It increases compression force on your joints thereby improving stability.

fields.description: Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son un ejercicio compuesto, ya que trabajan varios grupos musculares simultáneamente. Aunque este ejercicio de peso corporal se enfoca principalmente en los tríceps, también golpea el pecho y la parte delantera del hombro. Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son uno de los ejercicios más efectivos para aumentar la fuerza de los brazos y también desarrollar músculo magro en la parte superior de los brazos. Los fondos de tríceps en el suelo son un ejercicio de cadena cinética cerrada y expresan que haces los movimientos alrededor de un punto fijo. Aumenta la fuerza de compresión en las articulaciones, mejorando así la estabilidad.

Being a multi-functional movement, this exercise targets not only your abdominal muscles but also the spine and buttocks. fields.description: La plancha lateral es un entrenamiento eficaz para fortalecer el tronco. Puede ayudarlo a fortalecer y desarrollar los músculos de la parte superior e inferior del cuerpo. Al ser un movimiento multifuncional, este ejercicio se enfoca no solo en los músculos abdominales, sino también en la columna vertebral y los glúteos. La plancha fortalece y tensa todo el cuerpo, mejora la postura y el equilibrio, reduce la grasa corporal y puede ayudar a acelerar el metabolismo.

fields.description:
Połóż się na ławce, sztanga powinna znajdować się bezpośrednio nad twoimi oczami, kolana są ugięte, nogi w lekkim rozkroku a stopy mocno oparte na podłodze. Skoncentruj się, oddychaj prawidłowo i chwyć drążek szerzej niż na szerokość barków. Opuść go powoli, aż na chwilę dotknie klatki piersiowej na wysokości sutków. Popchnij sztangę do góry. Jeśli trenujesz z dużymi ciężarami, wskazane jest posiadanie asekuranta, który pomoże Ci podnieść sztangę, jeśli nie jesteś w stanie samodzielnie unieść ciężaru. Dzięki szerokości chwytu możesz także kontrolować, która część klatki piersiowej jest bardziej trenowana:

  • szeroki chwyt: zewnętrzne mięśnie klatki piersiowej
  • wąski chwyt: wewnętrzne mięśnie klatki piersiowej i triceps

fields.description:
The single arm cable lateral raise is a variation of the lateral raise and an exercise used to build the muscles of the shoulders.

  1. Position a cable at the lowest position possible and attach a single handle.
  2. Reach across your body and grab the handle with a neutral grip.
  3. Keep the elbow slightly bent and pull the handle across your body and raise laterally.
  4. Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position under control.

3. Keep the elbow slightly bent and pull the handle across your body and raise laterally.
4. Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position under control.

fields.description: Le mouvement est très similaire au développé couché avec une barre, mais le poids est ramené plus bas sur la poitrine. Tenez deux haltères et allongez-vous sur un banc. Tenez les haltères près de la poitrine et poussez-les vers le haut jusqu'à ce que les bras soient tendus. Laissez retomber les haltères lentement et de manière contrôlée.
fields.description_source:
Le mouvement est très similaire au développé couché avec une barre, mais le poids est ramené plus bas sur la poitrine.
Tenez deux haltères et allongez-vous sur un banc. Tenez les haltères près de la poitrine et poussez-les vers le haut jusqu'à ce que les bras soient tendus. Laissez retomber les haltères lentement et de manière contrôlée.
fields.name: Développé couché avec haltères

fields.description:
1. Commencez par prendre un haltère dans chaque main, les paumes tournées vers le torse. Gardez le dos droit, pliez légèrement les genoux et penchez-vous vers l'avant au niveau de la taille. Votre torse doit être presque parallèle au sol. Veillez à garder la tête haute. Les parties supérieures des bras doivent être proche du torse et parallèle au sol. Les avant-bras doivent être dirigés vers le sol lorsque vous tenez les haltères. Un angle de 90 degrés doit être formé entre l'avant-bras et le bras. C'est votre position de départ.
  2. Maintenant, tout en gardant les bras immobiles, expirez et utilisez vos triceps pour soulever les haltères jusqu'à ce que les bras soient complètement tendus. Concentrez-vous sur le mouvement de l'avant-bras.
  3. Après une brève pause au sommet de la contraction, inspirez et redescendez lentement les haltères jusqu'à la position de départ.
  4. Répétez le mouvement pour le nombre de répétitions prescrit.

Variantes :

  • Cet exercice peut être exécuté avec un seul bras à la fois.
  • De plus, si vous aimez la variante à un bras, vous pouvez utiliser une poulie basse au lieu d'un haltère pour une meilleure contraction maximale. Dans ce cas, les paumes doivent être tournées vers le haut plutôt que vers le torse.

fields.description:
1. Stand upright and hold weights in both hands if using loads.
  2. Brace your core and lift one leg off the ground.
  3. Keep your back straight, hinge at the hips while lowering your torso forward, ensuring you don’t rotate your hips.
  4. Lower until you feel a stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to standing position. Repeat on both sides.
fields.description_source:
1. Stand upright and hold weights in both hands if using loads.
2. Brace your core and lift one leg off the ground.
3. Keep your back straight, hinge at the hips while lowering your torso forward, ensuring you don’t rotate your hips.
4. Lower until you feel a stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to standing position. Repeat on both sides.
fields.name: Single Leg RDL

fields.description: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, shift your weight onto one leg and slightly bend the other. Keep your back straight while hinging at your hips. Use your hamstring to pull yourself back up.
