fields.name: Puxada Alta

fields.description:
1. Segurando uma barra com pegada pronada (palmas das mãos voltadas para baixo), dobre ligeiramente os joelhos e incline o tronco para a frente, flexionando a cintura, mantendo as costas retas até que fiquem quase paralelas ao chão. Dica: Certifique-se de manter a cabeça erguida. A barra deve ficar pendurada diretamente à sua frente, com os braços perpendiculares ao chão e ao tronco. Esta é a sua posição inicial.
  2. Agora, mantendo o tronco imóvel, expire e levante a barra em sua direção. Mantenha os cotovelos próximos ao corpo e use apenas os antebraços para sustentar o peso. Na posição de contração máxima, contraia os músculos das costas e mantenha a posição por uma breve pausa.
  3. Em seguida, inspire e abaixe lentamente a barra de volta à posição inicial.
  4. Repita o número de repetições recomendado.

fields.description:
sometric Squat to Failure: A Strength Training Technique

Definition

The isometric squat to failure is a variation of the squat exercise where you hold a static squat position at a specific angle, maintaining the position until your muscles can no longer sustain the contraction.

sometric Squat to Failure: A Strength Training Technique

Definition

fields.name: Isometric Squat to Failure

fields.description:
Sentadilla Isométrica al Fallo

Definición

La sentadilla isométrica al fallo es una variante de ejercicio de sentadilla donde mantienes una posición estática de sentadilla en un ángulo específico, sosteniendo la posición hasta que los músculos no pueden mantener más la contracción.

Tipo de Contracción Isométrica (estática, sin movimiento)

Grupos Musculares Principales Cuádriceps, glúteos, músculos posteriores del muslo

Intensidad Muy alta, trabaja hasta el agotamiento muscular

Beneficios Mejora fuerza, resistencia muscular, estabilidad

Técnica de Ejecución

Colócate en posición de sentadilla con los pies separados al ancho de las caderas

Desciende hasta formar un ángulo de 90 grados en las rodillas

Mantén esa posición completamente inmóvil

Resiste hasta que los músculos fallen y no puedas sostener más la posición

Beneficios

Desarrollo de fuerza máxima

Mejora de la resistencia muscular

Fortalecimiento de la estabilidad articular

Incremento de la tolerancia al dolor muscular

Precauciones

No recomendado para principiantes

Riesgo de fatiga muscular intensa

Consultar con un profesional antes de implementarlo

Usar con moderación en la rutina de entrenamiento
fields.description_source:

Definición

La sentadilla isométrica al fallo es una variante de ejercicio de sentadilla donde mantienes una posición estática de sentadilla en un ángulo específico, sosteniendo la posición hasta que los músculos no pueden mantener más la contracción.

Grupos Musculares Principales Cuádriceps, glúteos, músculos posteriores del muslo

fields.description:
How to do a single-leg kettlebell lunge

Preparation: Stand with your feet together. Hold the kettlebell with both hands by the handle, keeping it close to your chest, or hold it with one hand (on the same side as the working leg).

Step and Lower: Take a large step forward (or backward, which is safer for your knees) with one leg, lowering your hips until both knees form a 90-degree angle.

Focus: Shift your weight onto your front (or bent) leg, keeping your back straight and your torso upright.

Return: Push off with your front leg to return to the starting position.

Focus: Shift your weight onto your front (or bent) leg, keeping your back straight and your torso upright.

Return: Push off with your front leg to return to the starting position.

fields.description: La "zancada con pesa rusa a una pierna" (o unilateral) es un ejercicio que mejora el equilibrio y la fuerza, implicando sujetar la pesa rusa cerca del pecho o con un brazo (mismo lado de la pierna de apoyo) y bajar en una zancada profunda, alternando piernas, o hacer variantes como sentadillas búlgaras con kettlebell, enfocándose en la estabilidad y el trabajo unilateral del tren inferior para fortalecer cuádriceps, glúteos y femorales, con versiones hacia adelante, atrás o laterales.  Cómo hacer la zancada unilateral con pesa rusa Preparación: Ponte de pie con los pies juntos. Sujeta la pesa rusa con ambas manos por el asa, manteniéndola pegada al pecho, o sosténla con una sola mano (del mismo lado que la pierna que trabajará). Paso y descenso: Da un gran paso hacia adelante (o hacia atrás, que es más seguro para las rodillas) con una pierna, bajando la cadera hasta que ambas rodillas formen un ángulo de 90 grados. Enfoque: Desplaza el peso hacia la pierna delantera (o la que se flexiona), manteniendo la espalda recta y el torso erguido. Regreso: Empuja con la pierna delantera para volver a la posición inicial.
fields.description_source:

Preparación: Ponte de pie con los pies juntos. Sujeta la pesa rusa con ambas manos por el asa, manteniéndola pegada al pecho, o sosténla con una sola mano (del mismo lado que la pierna que trabajará).
Paso y descenso: Da un gran paso hacia adelante (o hacia atrás, que es más seguro para las rodillas) con una pierna, bajando la cadera hasta que ambas rodillas formen un ángulo de 90 grados.
Enfoque: Desplaza el peso hacia la pierna delantera (o la que se flexiona), manteniendo la espalda recta y el torso erguido.
Regreso: Empuja con la pierna delantera para volver a la posición inicial.

fields.description:
The Single-Leg Side Glute Press is a leg press machine variation where you angle your body sideways on the pad, placing one foot high and slightly angled to press the weight, powerfully targeting the outer glute (gluteus medius/minimus) and hamstrings for balanced leg development and stability, requiring focus on pushing through the heel and maintaining control without locking out.

How to Perform:

Setup: Sit in the leg press machine, rotate your body to one hip, placing one foot high on the platform at about a 45-degree angle with toes pointed slightly out/forward.

Positioning: Keep your hip, knee, and ankle aligned, and ensure your lower back stays on the pad.

Descent: Slowly lower the weight, allowing your knee to bend deeply (near 90 degrees), feeling a stretch in your glute and hamstring.

Press: Drive through your heel (not your toes) to press the weight back up, squeezing your glutes and hamstrings, stopping just before your leg fully extends.

Control: Avoid bouncing and don't let your lower back lift off the pad; maintain tension throughout the movement.

fields.description:
Colócate en la máquina de prensa de piernas, pero en lugar de sentarte de frente, siéntate de lado para que la pierna que vas a trabajar quede alineada con la plataforma móvil. Asegura tu espalda contra el respaldo y agárrate bien de las asas para mantener la estabilidad

Coloca el pie de la pierna de trabajo en la plataforma, asegurándote de que el talón quede bien apoyado y que la pierna esté casi completamente extendida, pero sin bloquear la rodilla. Desbloquea la seguridad de la máquina. Baja la plataforma lentamente y con control, doblando la rodilla y la cadera. El objetivo es llevar el talón lo más cerca posible de tu glúteo sin que la zona lumbar se despegue del respaldo. Es crucial mantener la espalda recta y no encorvarse.

Empuja la plataforma hacia arriba usando principalmente la fuerza del glúteo y el cuádriceps, volviendo a la posición inicial (pierna casi extendida). Concéntrate en la contracción muscular en la parte superior del movimiento

Evita rebotar o usar el impulso

Comienza con un peso ligero para dominar la técnica y evitar lesiones, especialmente en la espalda baja o las rodillas.

fields.description:
Starting Position: Stand upright with a slight bend in your knees, holding a dumbbell in one or both hands. Shift your weight onto your supporting leg (e.g., your right leg) and lift your non-supporting foot slightly off the floor.

The Hinge: Keeping your back flat and core engaged, hinge at your hips, pushing your glutes backward. As your torso lowers, extend your non-supporting leg straight back behind you for balance, keeping your hips square to the floor. The dumbbell(s) should lower toward the ground along the line of your supporting leg.

Range of Motion: Continue lowering your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, or you feel an intense stretch in your supporting hamstring, ensuring your back remains neutral throughout the movement.

Return to Start: Pause at the bottom, then contract your glutes and hamstrings to slowly raise your torso back to the starting position. Your non-supporting leg should return in line with the supporting one.

Switch Sides: After completing your desired number of repetitions on one side, switch your weight and repeat the movement on the other leg.

fields.description:
Postura inicial:

Sostén una mancuerna en la mano opuesta a la pierna que vas a utilizar como soporte. (Si te paras sobre la pierna derecha, sostén la mancuerna con la mano izquierda). Esto ayuda a contrarrestar el peso y activa los músculos estabilizadores.

Mantén la espalda recta y el core activado.

Mueve ligeramente el peso de tu cuerpo a la pierna de apoyo, con una mínima flexión en la rodilla.

El movimiento (la bisagra de cadera):

Inicia el movimiento doblando la cadera hacia atrás, no doblando las rodillas. Imagina que empujas un interruptor con los glúteos.

Simultáneamente, extiende la pierna libre hacia atrás para mantener el equilibrio. Tu cuerpo debería formar una línea recta desde la cabeza hasta el talón de la pierna extendida.

Baja la mancuerna hacia el suelo, manteniendo el brazo extendido. El objetivo es que tu torso quede casi paralelo al suelo, o hasta donde tu flexibilidad te lo permita sin curvar la espalda baja.

El regreso:

Haz una pausa breve en la parte inferior del movimiento.

Empuja el suelo con el talón de la pierna de apoyo y contrae los glúteos para volver a la posición inicial, llevando la pierna trasera de vuelta al lado de la de apoyo.

Mantén el control en todo momento.

fields.description:
Machine side lateral raises are an isolation exercise for the side (lateral) deltoids, performed on a machine for stability, targeting the outer shoulder by lifting pads or handles out to the sides to shoulder height, then lowering slowly, keeping elbows slightly bent and traps down to focus on the delts. This exercise helps build shoulder width by preventing swinging and providing constant tension, making it great for beginners or as a finisher.

How to Perform Machine Lateral Raises

Setup: Adjust the seat so your shoulders align with the machine's pivot point, your chest is against the pad (or back if facing out), and your arms are at a 90-degree angle with the pads/handles.

Starting Position: Grip the handles with a relaxed grip, elbows slightly bent, and shoulders down (depressed) away from your ears.

Lifting Phase: Exhale and lift your arms out to the sides, moving your upper arms laterally, until they are parallel to the floor.

Peak Contraction: Pause briefly at the top, squeezing the side delts, but don't shrug your shoulders up.

Lowering Phase: Inhale and slowly lower the weight back down with control, resisting the weight on the way down.

fields.description:
Técnica de Ejecución

La elevación lateral en máquina es un ejercicio diseñado para trabajar específicamente el músculo deltoides lateral (hombro medio) con un alto grado de control y seguridad.

Pasos de Ejecución:

Ajuste la máquina a su altura y posición correcta

Siéntese con la espalda apoyada firmemente contra el respaldo

Agarre los mangos laterales con un agarre cómodo

Mantenga los codos ligeramente flexionados

Eleve los brazos lateralmente hasta la altura de los hombros

Pause brevemente en la posición superior

Baje los brazos de manera controlada a la posición inicial

Beneficios

Aislamiento preciso del deltoides lateral

Menor riesgo de lesiones en comparación con elevaciones libres

Control del rango de movimiento

Ideal para principiantes y rehabilitación

Consejos Importantes

Evite balancear o hacer movimientos de impulso

Mantenga un movimiento controlado y constante

No sobrecargue el peso

Respire de manera apropiada: exhale al levantar, inhale al bajar

fields.description:
The Cable Front Raise with a small bar isolates your anterior deltoids (front shoulders) by providing constant tension, requiring you to stand facing away from a low pulley, grab the bar with an overhand grip, and lift it in front of you to shoulder height with slightly bent arms, avoiding swinging and keeping your core tight for controlled movement up and down. Use a light-to-moderate weight, focusing on slow, controlled reps to feel the muscle work, not momentum.

How to Perform the Cable Front Raise (Bar)

Setup: Attach a small straight bar to a low cable pulley. Set the weight to a light or moderate setting.

Starting Position: Stand with your back to the machine, holding the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) at hip level, arms extended but not locked. Feet shoulder-width apart, core braced, shoulders back and down.

The Lift: Exhale and slowly raise the bar straight up in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until your hands reach shoulder height (or slightly below).

The Hold: Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your front delts.

The Lower: Inhale as you slowly and controllably lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension.
fields.description_source:

Use a light-to-moderate weight, focusing on slow, controlled reps to feel the muscle work, not momentum.

How to Perform the Cable Front Raise (Bar)

Setup: Attach a small straight bar to a low cable pulley. Set the weight to a light or moderate setting.

Starting Position: Stand with your back to the machine, holding the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down) at hip level, arms extended but not locked. Feet shoulder-width apart, core braced, shoulders back and down.

The Lift: Exhale and slowly raise the bar straight up in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until your hands reach shoulder height (or slightly below).

The Hold: Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your front delts.

The Lower: Inhale as you slowly and controllably lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension.
fields.name: Cable Front Raise with a small bar

fields.description:
Posición inicial: Colócate de espaldas a la polea baja. Agarra la barra recta (o barra Z) con un agarre prono (palmas hacia abajo), a la anchura de los hombros. Da un paso atrás para que la barra quede a la altura de tus muslos, con las rodillas ligeramente flexionadas y el core activado para proteger la zona lumbar.

Fase de elevación: Levanta la barra hacia adelante en un arco, manteniendo los codos semiflexionados. Sube hasta que la barra esté a la altura de los hombros o un poco por debajo.

Contracción: En la parte superior, aprieta el deltoides anterior (parte frontal del hombro) durante un instante.

Fase de descenso: Baja el peso de forma lenta y controlada hasta la posición inicial. La fase excéntrica (bajada) es crucial para el desarrollo muscular.

Consejos clave

Evita el balanceo: Mantén el torso firme y no uses el impulso del cuerpo. Si te balanceas, el peso es demasiado alto.

Codos: Mantenlos ligeramente flexionados y fijos; no los extiendas por completo ni los subas excesivamente.

Enfoque: Concéntrate en sentir el trabajo en el deltoides anterior. Es un movimiento de flexión de hombro, no de bíceps.

fields.description:
Technique and Execution

The Smith machine squat is a variation of the traditional barbell squat that uses a guided, fixed-path barbell. Here's a detailed breakdown of the proper technique:

Starting Position

Foot Placement: Position feet slightly forward of the bar, about shoulder-width apart

Bar Position: Rest the bar on your upper trapezius/shoulders, similar to a traditional back squat

Stance: Feet can be slightly angled outward for natural hip rotation

Squat Movement

Unrack the bar by rotating it to release from the safety hooks

Slowly lower your body by bending knees and hips

Descend until thighs are parallel to the ground (or slightly below)

Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement

Drive through your heels to return to the starting position

Benefits and Considerations

Advantages

Reduced Balance Requirements: Fixed bar path makes it easier for beginners

Controlled Movement: Less risk of improper form compared to free weight squats

Isolation: Allows focused leg muscle development
fields.description_source:
Technique and Execution

The Smith machine squat is a variation of the traditional barbell squat that uses a guided, fixed-path barbell. Here's a detailed breakdown of the proper technique:

Starting Position

Foot Placement: Position feet slightly forward of the bar, about shoulder-width apart

Bar Position: Rest the bar on your upper trapezius/shoulders, similar to a traditional back squat

Stance: Feet can be slightly angled outward for natural hip rotation

Squat Movement

Unrack the bar by rotating it to release from the safety hooks

Slowly lower your body by bending knees and hips

Descend until thighs are parallel to the ground (or slightly below)

Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement

Drive through your heels to return to the starting position

Benefits and Considerations

Advantages

Reduced Balance Requirements: Fixed bar path makes it easier for beginners

Controlled Movement: Less risk of improper form compared to free weight squats

Isolation: Allows focused leg muscle development
fields.name: Smith machine squat

fields.description:
Técnica y ejecución

La sentadilla en máquina Smith es una variante de la sentadilla con barra tradicional que utiliza una barra guiada con una trayectoria fija. Aquí tienes un desglose detallado de la técnica correcta:

Posición inicial

Colocación de los pies: Coloca los pies ligeramente por delante de la barra, separados aproximadamente a la anchura de los hombros.

Posición de la barra: Apoya la barra sobre la parte superior del trapecio/hombros, similar a una sentadilla tradicional.

Postura: Los pies pueden estar ligeramente inclinados hacia afuera para una rotación natural de la cadera.

Movimiento de la sentadilla

Descarga la barra girándola para soltarla de los ganchos de seguridad.

Baja lentamente el cuerpo flexionando las rodillas y las caderas.

Desciende hasta que los muslos estén paralelos al suelo (o ligeramente por debajo).

Haz una breve pausa al final del movimiento.

Impulsa los talones para volver a la posición inicial.

Beneficios y consideraciones

Ventajas

Requiere menos equilibrio: La trayectoria fija de la barra facilita el movimiento para principiantes.

Movimiento controlado: Menor riesgo de mala postura en comparación con las sentadillas con peso libre.

Aislamiento: Permite el desarrollo muscular de las piernas.

fields.name: Sentadilla multipower

fields.description:
Technique and Execution

Starting Position

Seated Positioning: Sit in the machine with back against the pad

Leg Placement: Inner thighs against the machine's padded resistance points

Adjust Machine: Set seat position to align your hip joint with the machine's pivot point

Movement Technique

Start with legs together or slightly pressed against the inner pads

Slowly push legs outward, spreading them apart

Move until maximum comfortable lateral range is reached

Pause briefly at the outer point of the movement

Slowly return to the starting position with controlled movement

Biomechanical Breakdown

Muscle Engagement

Primary Activation: Gluteus Medius (side hip muscle)

Stabilization: Engages core and lower back muscles

Functional Movement: Mimics lateral leg movement used in walking, sports, and daily activities

Benefits

Strength and Stability

Improves Hip Stability

Reduces Risk of Knee Injuries

Enhances Lateral Movement Capabilities

Targets Often-Neglected Muscle Groups

fields.name: Machine Hip Abduction

fields.description:
Técnica y ejecución

Posición inicial

Posición sentada: Siéntese en la máquina con la espalda apoyada en la almohadilla. Colocación de las piernas: La parte interna de los muslos contra los puntos de resistencia acolchados de la máquina. Ajuste de la máquina: Ajuste la posición del asiento para alinear la articulación de la cadera con el punto de pivote de la máquina.

Técnica de movimiento

Comience con las piernas juntas o ligeramente presionadas contra las almohadillas internas. Empuje lentamente las piernas hacia afuera, separándolas. Muévase hasta alcanzar el máximo rango lateral cómodo. Haga una breve pausa en el punto final del movimiento. Regrese lentamente a la posición inicial con un movimiento controlado.

Desglose biomecánico

Activación muscular

Activación principal: Glúteo medio (músculo lateral de la cadera)

Estabilización: Activa los músculos del core y la zona lumbar.

Movimiento funcional: Imita el movimiento lateral de las piernas utilizado al caminar, practicar deportes y realizar actividades diarias.

Beneficios

Fuerza y ​​estabilidad

Mejora la estabilidad de la cadera

Reduce el riesgo de lesiones de rodilla

Mejora la capacidad de movimiento lateral

Trabaja grupos musculares a menudo descuidados

Rehabilitación y prevención

Beneficioso para:

Corredores

Atletas de deportes de movimiento lateral

Personas que se recuperan de lesiones en la parte inferior del cuerpo

Prevención de la desalineación de cadera y rodilla

fields.name: Abducción en máquina

fields.description:
Machine Setup

Adjust the seat height so that the machine's handles align with your elbow joint

Ensure your back is firmly supported against the backrest

Feet should be flat on the floor

Maintain a neutral spine position

Movement Mechanics

Starting Position

Grip the handles with a neutral grip

Keep upper arms close to your body

Elbows bent at approximately 90 degrees

Press Movement

Extend arms forward by pushing the handles away

Focus on using triceps to drive the movement

Avoid using shoulder or chest muscles

Fully extend arms without locking elbows

Return Phase

Slowly return to the starting position

Maintain controlled, smooth motion

Keep tension on the triceps throughout

fields.name: Seated Triceps Press

fields.description:
El press de tríceps sentado es un ejercicio de entrenamiento de resistencia específico, diseñado específicamente para desarrollar y fortalecer los tríceps.

Ajuste la altura del asiento para que las manijas de la máquina se alineen con la articulación del codo

Asegúrese de que la espalda esté firmemente apoyada contra el respaldo

Los pies deben estar apoyados en el suelo

Mantenga una posición neutra de la columna

Posición inicial

Sujete las manijas con un agarre neutro

Mantenga los brazos pegados al cuerpo

Codos doblados aproximadamente a 90 grados

Movimiento de prensa

Extienda los brazos hacia adelante empujando las manijas hacia afuera

Concéntrese en usar los tríceps para impulsar el movimiento

Evite usar los músculos del hombro o el pecho

Extienda completamente los brazos sin bloquear los codos

Regrese lentamente a la posición inicial

Mantenga un movimiento controlado y suave

Mantenga la tensión en los tríceps durante todo el ejercicio

fields.description: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand and bending forward at the hips while keeping the back straight, then returning to a standing position. It primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes.
fields.description_source: This exercise involves holding a dumbbell in each hand and bending forward at the hips while keeping the back straight, then returning to a standing position. It primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes.
fields.name: Romanian Deadlift

fields.description:
The Cable Pull Through is a lower-body exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings using a "hip hinge" motion. To perform it, attach a rope to the lowest pulley setting, face away from the machine with the rope between your legs, and step forward to create tension. Keeping your back flat and knees slightly soft, push your hips backward as if trying to close a door behind you until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.

To finish the rep, explosively drive your hips forward to return to a standing position, squeezing your glutes hard at the top. It is vital to remember that this is not a squat; your knees should not bend deeply, and your arms should remain straight and relaxed, acting only as hooks for the rope. This movement provides constant tension on the glutes while placing less stress on the lower back than a traditional deadlift.
fields.description_source:
The **Cable Pull Through** is a lower-body exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings using a "hip hinge" motion. To perform it, attach a rope to the lowest pulley setting, face away from the machine with the rope between your legs, and step forward to create tension. Keeping your back flat and knees slightly soft, push your hips backward as if trying to close a door behind you until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.

To finish the rep, explosively drive your hips forward to return to a standing position, squeezing your glutes hard at the top. It is vital to remember that this is **not a squat**; your knees should not bend deeply, and your arms should remain straight and relaxed, acting only as hooks for the rope. This movement provides constant tension on the glutes while placing less stress on the lower back than a traditional deadlift.
fields.name: Cable pull through

Não dê seu corpo equilibrar durante o exercício, todo o trabalho é feito pelos bíceps, que são os humanos múltiplos que devem se mover (preste atenção aos cotovelos).

fields.description:
The Pull-Up is a bodyweight exercise that builds strength in your back, shoulders, and biceps. It can be difficult at first, but you can make it easier by using bands or an assisted machine.

Instructions
  1. Grab the bar: Place your hands on the bar with your palms facing away from you.
  2. Get ready: Take a breath and tighten your stomach muscles.
  3. Pull up: Pull your body up until your chest reaches the bar. Think about driving your elbows down.
  4. Lower down: Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
fields.description_source:
The Pull-Up is a bodyweight exercise that builds strength in your back, shoulders, and biceps. It can be difficult at first, but you can make it easier by using bands or an assisted machine.

### **Instructions**

1. **Grab the bar:** Place your hands on the bar with your palms facing away from you.
2. **Get ready:** Take a breath and tighten your stomach muscles.
3. **Pull up:** Pull your body up until your chest reaches the bar. Think about driving your elbows down.
4. **Lower down:** Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
fields.name: Pull-Ups (Wide Grip)

fields.description:
Starting position:

Sit down on the front edge of a chair, back straight, hands holding the front edge. Still holding the edge of the chair, arms extended, lift your butt and walk forward slightly so that it is a few inches from the chair.

Steps:

  1. Slowly lower your body, keeping the back straight, until your arms are at a right angle.

  2. Raise your body again to the initial position, arms extended.

  3. Repeat.

fields.description:
• The barbell full squat is a compound exercise that targets multiple muscle groups in the lower body, including the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes.
  • Proper form is crucial for maximizing results and preventing injuries during the barbell full squat. This includes maintaining a shoulder-width stance, creating whole body tension, controlling your descent, and maintaining proper depth and knee positioning.
  • Assistance moves such as the front squat, goblet squat, split squat, and Bulgarian split squat can help improve your performance in the barbell full squat by targeting specific muscle groups and improving overall technique.
  • To achieve new personal records in your back squat, gradually increase weight over time, vary rep ranges and sets to stimulate muscle growth, and prioritize rest and recovery between training sessions.
fields.description_source:
* The barbell full squat is a **compound exercise** that **targets multiple muscle groups** in the lower body, including the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes.
* Proper form is crucial for maximizing results and preventing injuries during the barbell full squat. This includes maintaining a shoulder-width stance, creating whole body tension, controlling your descent, and maintaining proper depth and knee positioning.
* Assistance moves such as the front squat, goblet squat, split squat, and Bulgarian split squat can help improve your performance in the barbell full squat by targeting specific muscle groups and improving overall technique.
* To **achieve new personal records** in your back squat, gradually increase weight over time, vary rep ranges and sets to stimulate muscle growth, and **prioritize rest and recovery** between training sessions.
fields.name: Barbell Full Squat

fields.description:
Segure dois halteres e sente-se em um banco com as costas retas, os ombros estão ligados rolados para trás. Seus amigos apostam para seu corpo. Dobre os braços e levante o peso com um movimento rápido. Não gire como homens, como acontece com os cachos. Sem nenhuma pausa, baixar o haltere com um movimento lento e controlado.

Não balance o corpo durante o exercício, o bíceps deve fazer todo o trabalho aqui. Os cotovelos estão ao seu lado e não se movem.
fields.description_source:
Segure dois halteres e sente-se em um banco com as costas retas, os ombros estão ligados rolados para trás. Seus amigos apostam para seu corpo. Dobre os braços e levante o peso com um movimento rápido. Não gire como homens, como acontece com os cachos. Sem nenhuma pausa, baixar o haltere com um movimento lento e controlado.

Não balance o corpo durante o exercício, o bíceps deve fazer todo o trabalho aqui. Os cotovelos estão ao seu lado e não se movem.
fields.name: Biceps Martelo

fields.description: A barbell squat is a fundamental strength exercise where you lower your body as if sitting in a chair while holding a weighted barbell, primarily targeting leg and core muscles, requiring proper form (chest up, back straight, knees over toes) for safety and effectiveness, engaging quads, hamstrings, glutes, and core for strength, muscle, and fat reduction.

fields.description: Con el cuerpo ligeramente inclinado hacia atrás y el agarre un poco más abierto de la altura de los hombros, tirar la barra hacia el pecho.
fields.description_source: Con el cuerpo ligeramente inclinado hacia atrás y el agarre un poco más abierto de la altura de los hombros, tirar la barra hacia el pecho.
fields.name: Jalón al pecho (inclinado)

fields.description:
La ejecución de este ejercicio es muy similar a la del remo inclinado normal, solo que aquí la barra está fija.

  1. Agarra la barra con un agarre amplio (ligeramente más ancho que los hombros) e inclínate hacia delante. La parte superior del cuerpo no debe quedar totalmente paralela al suelo, sino formar un ligero ángulo. Mantén el pecho hacia fuera durante todo el ejercicio.
  2. Tira de la barra con un movimiento rápido hacia el ombligo, sin subirla más.
  3. Vuelve lentamente a la posición inicial.

No balancees el cuerpo y mantén los brazos pegados al cuerpo.

fields.description:
1. Stand between a set of parallel bars.
  2. Place your hands, knuckles outwards, on the bars.
  3. Push down on your hands to lift yourself off the floor.
  4. Hold yourself in this position with your arms straight for the required time.

fields.description:
Lie face-down on a bench with your legs hanging off the edge.

  1. Bend your hips so your thighs are vertical, bending your knees at the same time so your shins stay horizontal.
  2. Straighten out your legs so they are not bent and your toes are pointing backwards horizontally.
  3. Return to the original position, and repeat.

fields.description: Lie on your stomach flat, legs extended and the backs of your feet on the ground. Bring your forearms under your shoulders, elbows directly underneath. Then press your hands into the floor to lift your chest. Draw your shoulder blades together, open your collarbones, and direct your gaze forward, while keeping the lower back relaxed.

fields.description:
Basic exercise

  • Focus: enhances thoracic mobility and spinal segmentation while opening the shoulders and chest.
  • **Position:**kneeling on the floor with your right side facing a wall. Hips are square and stable, with the right knee down and the left foot forward. Lengthen your spine and align your head comfortably. Begin with your arms relaxed and open in front of your chest, palms facing each other and hands apart.
  • Inhale to initiate the movement, allowing your left arm —the one farther from the wall— to open outward and backward. Lead the twist from your thoracic spine, rotating your upper back progressively while keeping your pelvis still.
  • Exhale as you complete the rotation, guiding your left hand toward the wall as your gaze follows your hand.
  • Return by inhaling and slowly bringing your arm forward, reversing the spinal rotation segment by segment.
  • Repeat on the opposite side.

Progression

  • **Position:**kneeling on the floor with your right side facing a wall. Left knee down and right foot forward.
  • Before the basic exercise: inhale, exhalewhile moving the right arm along the wall with a circle upwards and backwards, inhalewhile bringing back the arm.
  • Basic exercise.
  • Repeat on the opposite side.
fields.description_source:
**Basic exercise**

* **Focus:** enhances thoracic mobility and
  spinal segmentation while opening the shoulders and chest.
* **Position:**kneeling on the floor with your right side facing a wall. Hips are square and stable, with the right knee down and the left foot forward. Lengthen your spine and align your head comfortably.
  Begin with your arms relaxed and open in front of your chest, palms
  facing each other and hands apart.
* **Inhale**
  to initiate the movement, allowing your left arm —the one farther from
  the wall— to open outward and backward. Lead the twist from your thoracic
  spine, rotating your upper back progressively while keeping your pelvis
  still.
* **Exhale** as you complete the rotation, guiding your left hand
  toward the wall as your gaze follows your hand.
* **Return by inhaling** and
  slowly bringing your arm forward, reversing the spinal rotation segment
  by segment.
* **Repeat on the opposite side.**

**Progression**


* **Position:**kneeling on the floor with your right side facing a wall. Left knee down and right foot forward.
* **Before the basic exercise: inhale**, **exhale**while moving the right arm along the wall with a circle upwards and backwards, **inhale**while bringing back the arm.
* **Basic exercise.**
* **Repeat on the opposite side.**

Execution Lift your upper knee while keeping your feet close together, like a “shell opening,” then slowly return to the starting position. Keep your core stable and your hips aligned.

Plank laterale, avambraccio a terra, corpo in linea retta.

fields.description:
1. Set Up: Lie flat with a dumbbell in each hand. Extend your arms straight up over your chest as if you were at the top of a dumbbell bench press
  2. Starting Position: Position the dumbbells so they are touching or very close together with a pronated grip (palms facing your feet and thumbs facing each other)
  3. Lowering: Without moving your upper arms or shoulders, bend your elbows and flare them outward to the sides. Lower the inner ends of the dumbbells toward the center of your chest
  4. Touch and Pause: Gently touch the dumbbells to your chest. Do not rest the weight on your body; maintain constant tension in your triceps
  5. Press: Forcefully extend your elbows to return the dumbbells to the starting position. Focus on "pushing your pinkies toward the sky" to maximize triceps engagement

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IgSjoXbpy1M&t=2

fields.description: "The Y"Position: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie face-down with your chest supported, keeping your head neutral or looking slightly down to avoid neck strain.Grip: Hold light dumbbells with a neutral or thumbs-up grip (thumb pointing to the ceiling).Movement: With straight or slightly bent elbows, raise the dumbbells up and out in a "Y" shape. Focus on moving the shoulders and shoulder blades rather than just lifting the arms.Top Position: Squeeze the shoulder blades together and reach high, ensuring the arms are angled to form a "Y".Control: Lower the dumbbells slowly to the starting position.

fields.description:
Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, chest out, and shoulders back.

Hold the resistance band with both hands, palms facing up or facing each other, depending on the band type.

Bend your elbows at a 90-degree angle and glue your upper arms to your sides.

The band should have some tension in the starting position, with your hands closer together in front of your stomach.

Rotate Outward: While keeping your elbows tucked into your sides, slowly move your hands away from each other, stretching the band.

Squeeze: Squeeze your shoulder blades together and down as you rotate your hands outward.

Range of Motion: Rotate your hands as far outward as you comfortably can without allowing your elbows to leave your sides.

Hold: Hold the end position for 1–2 seconds to maximize muscle engagement.

Return: Slowly and with control, bring your hands back to the starting position.

fields.description:
Lie on your back with knees and hips bent at 90 degrees (tabletop).

Place your hands on your thighs just above your knees. Push your hands against your knees as hard as possible while using your abs to pull your knees toward your hands.

Hold for 10 seconds of maximum effort. Your abs should be shaking.

Place your hands on your thighs just above your knees. Push your hands against your knees as hard as possible while using your abs to pull your knees toward your hands.

Hold for 10 seconds of maximum effort. Your abs should be shaking.

fields.name: Abdominal Draw-In

fields.description:
Bar Position: Anchor one end of a barbell in a landmine attachment or a secure corner. Stance: Stand facing the barbell with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Turn your toes out 10–35 degrees to allow for better pelvic movement and depth. Grip: Cup the free end of the barbell with both hands (fingers interlaced or overlapping) and hold it at mid-chest level, just below the collarbone.

Descent: Brace your core and sit back into a squat, keeping your chest up and weight on your heels. Depth: Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor. A good reference is when your elbows touch the tops of your thighs or just inside your knees. Posture: Ensure your back remains flat and your spine neutral throughout the movement.

Drive: Powerfully drive through your heels to stand up. Use the momentum from your legs to "thrust" the weight upward. Extension: In one fluid motion, press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended. At the top, your biceps should be near your ears. The "Lean": As you reach the top of the press, lean slightly forward into the bar to encourage proper upward rotation of the shoulder blades and avoid lower back arching.

Slowly lower the barbell back to chest height in a controlled manner before immediately beginning the next repetition.
fields.description_source:
Bar Position: Anchor one end of a barbell in a landmine attachment or a secure corner.  
Stance: Stand facing the barbell with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Turn your toes out 10–35 degrees to allow for better pelvic movement and depth.  
Grip: Cup the free end of the barbell with both hands (fingers interlaced or overlapping) and hold it at mid-chest level, just below the collarbone.   
  
Descent: Brace your core and sit back into a squat, keeping your chest up and weight on your heels.  
Depth: Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor. A good reference is when your elbows touch the tops of your thighs or just inside your knees.  
Posture: Ensure your back remains flat and your spine neutral throughout the movement.   
  
Drive: Powerfully drive through your heels to stand up. Use the momentum from your legs to "thrust" the weight upward.  
Extension: In one fluid motion, press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended. At the top, your biceps should be near your ears.  
The "Lean": As you reach the top of the press, lean slightly forward into the bar to encourage proper upward rotation of the shoulder blades and avoid lower back arching.   
  
Slowly lower the barbell back to chest height in a controlled manner before immediately beginning the next repetition.

fields.description: Step 1: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell on your upper back. Step 2: Keep your back straight, chest up, and core engaged. Step 3: Take a step back with one leg and lower your body by bending both knees. Ensure that your back knee nearly touches the ground. Step 4: Push off your back foot to return to the starting position. Step 5: Repeat the movement with the other leg. Continue alternating between legs for the desired repetitions.
fields.description_source:

Step 2: Keep your back straight, chest up, and core engaged.
Step 3: Take a step back with one leg and lower your body by bending both knees. Ensure that your back knee nearly touches the ground.
Step 4: Push off your back foot to return to the starting position.
Step 5: Repeat the movement with the other leg.
Continue alternating between legs for the desired repetitions.
fields.name: Barbell Step Back Lunge

fields.description:
1. Sit upright in a hammerstrength decline chest press machine while squeezing your shoulder blades together and placing your heels firmly on the ground underneath your knees.
  2. The back pad should be contact with your head, shoulders, and butt at all times.
  3. Grab ahold of the handles with an overhand grip just outside shoulder-width apart just below your chest.
  4. Keeping your core braced by breathing into your stomach and flexing the abdominal muscles, push through your palms to extend your elbows while keeping them at a 45 degree angle from your torso.
  5. Once your arms are fully extended, return to the starting position.

4. Keeping your core braced by breathing into your stomach and flexing the abdominal muscles, push through your palms to extend your elbows while keeping them at a 45 degree angle from your torso.
5. Once your arms are fully extended, return to the starting position.

fields.description:
1. Sit with your feet pressed against each other, and one knee raised all the way out to the side of your body.
  2. Place your elbow on your raised knee, bend it at 90 degrees, and point your hand directly forward, holding your barbell/dumbbell.
  3. Keep your shoulder depressed and retracted, raise your hand until your forearm is vertical, as if you were about to wave at someone like a robot.
  4. Lower your hand to the original position and repeat.

fields.description: In Rückenlage, mit den Händen über dem Kopf an etwas festhalten (Kopfpolster der Hantelbank, unterste Sprosse der Sprossenwand...), sodass der Oberkörper möglichst unbeweglich wird. Dann die Beine in Richtung Decke heben, dabei auch das Becken abheben (ähnlich wie die "Kerze" aus Gymnastik/Yoga). Die Beine langsam wieder ablassen, möglichst viel Widerstand aus dem Bauch heraus. Beine seitlich drehen ist auch möglich.

Dann die Beine in Richtung Decke heben, dabei auch das Becken abheben (ähnlich wie die "Kerze" aus Gymnastik/Yoga). Die Beine langsam wieder ablassen, möglichst viel Widerstand aus dem Bauch heraus. fields.name: Dragon Flag

fields.description:
1. Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit facing the bench, chest against the pad, with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
  2. Position your feet firmly on the ground for stability and let your arms hang down naturally with a slight bend in your elbows.
  3. Engage your core and keep your back straight throughout the movement.
  4. Raise your arms forward and out to form a Y shape, keeping the movement controlled and your elbows slightly bent.
  5. Lift until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor and in line with your ears.
  6. Pause briefly at the top of the movement to engage your shoulder muscles fully, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
  7. Ensure the movement is slow and controlled during both the ascent and descent to maximize engagement of the deltoids and supporting muscles.
  8. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout.
fields.description_source:

4. Raise your arms forward and out to form a Y shape, keeping the movement controlled and your elbows slightly bent.
5. Lift until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor and in line with your ears.
6. Pause

   fully, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
7. Ensure

   to maximize engagement of the deltoids and supporting muscles.
8. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining proper form throughout.
fields.name: Incline DB Y-Raise

fields.description:
1. Using a pronated grip, grasp the pull bar with a wider than shoulder width grip.
  2. Take a deep breath, squeeze your glutes and brace your abs. Depress the shoulder blades and then drive the elbows straight down to the floor while activating the lats.
  3. Pull your chin towards the bar until the lats are fully contracted, then slowly lower yourself back to the start position and repeat for the assigned number of repetitions.

: Grab two dumbbells with an overhand grip and lay your forearms across your knees.

: Let your wrists flex fully, then extend your wrists.

fields.description:
Stand next to a cable machine with the pulley set to waist height.

Grab the opposite-side handle with the hand farthest from the pulley, keeping your palm facing in.

Keep your core engaged, chest lifted, and shoulders relaxed. Maintain a slight bend in the elbow throughout the lift.

Lift your arm out to the side until your elbow reaches shoulder height, keeping your wrist aligned with your elbow.

Briefly pause at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to your side with control. Repeat for the desired reps.

fields.name: High-Cable Lateral Raise

fields.description:
1. Adjust the seat height so that the grips are parallel to your chest.
  2. Use your legs to push the foot forward pedal.
  3. Bring the handles to the start position.
  4. Keep your back and the cushion in contact with the handles, press outwards.
  5. Press the handles away from your chest and exhale.
  6. Allow the handles to come in gently until they reach your chest.
  7. Allow the handles to return to your chest as you exhale.

fields.name: Machine Chest Press

fields.description:
• Starting Position: Sit on the floor with both hips and knees bent at 90-degree angles.
  • Leg Placement: Place one leg in front of you (external rotation) and the other leg out to the side/behind you (internal rotation). The shins and knees should remain flat on the ground.
  • The Movement: Keeping your hips square, hinge your torso forward or tilt it laterally (side-to-side) toward the front leg to deepen the stretch.
  • Target Areas: Stretches the glutes and piriformis (front leg) and the hip flexors and quads (back leg). Engages the core and obliques during lateral flexion.
  • Goal: Improves hip mobility, specifically internal and external rotation, and relieves lower back tension.
fields.description_source:
* **Starting Position:** Sit on the floor with both hips and knees bent at 90-degree angles.
* **Leg Placement:**
  Place one leg in front of you (external rotation) and the other leg out
  to the side/behind you (internal rotation). The shins and knees should
  remain flat on the ground.
* **The Movement:**
  Keeping your hips square, hinge your torso forward or tilt it laterally
  (side-to-side) toward the front leg to deepen the stretch.
* **Target Areas:**
  Stretches the glutes and piriformis (front leg) and the hip flexors and
  quads (back leg). Engages the core and obliques during lateral flexion.
* **Goal:** Improves hip mobility, specifically internal and external rotation, and relieves lower back tension.
fields.name: Shinbox IR Stretch

fields.description:
• Posizione di partenza: Seduti a terra con entrambe le anche e le ginocchia piegate a 90 gradi.
  • Posizione delle gambe: Una gamba è posizionata davanti (rotazione esterna), l'altra è posizionata di lato/dietro (rotazione interna). Tibie e ginocchia devono rimanere appoggiate a terra.
  • Il movimento: Mantenendo il bacino dritto, inclinare il busto in avanti o lateralmente (da un lato all'altro) verso la gamba anteriore per intensificare l'allungamento.
  • Aree coinvolte: Allunga i glutei e il piriforme (gamba anteriore) e i flessori dell'anca e i quadricipiti (gamba posteriore). Attiva il core e gli obliqui durante la flessione laterale.
  • Obiettivo: Migliora la mobilità dell'anca (rotazione interna ed esterna) e allevia la tensione nella zona lombare.
fields.description_source:
* **Posizione di partenza:** Seduti a terra con entrambe le anche e le ginocchia piegate a 90 gradi.
* **Posizione delle gambe:**
  Una gamba è posizionata davanti (rotazione esterna), l'altra è
  posizionata di lato/dietro (rotazione interna). Tibie e ginocchia devono

* **Il movimento:**
  Mantenendo il bacino dritto, inclinare il busto in avanti o

* **Aree coinvolte:**

* **Obiettivo:** Migliora la mobilità dell'anca (rotazione interna ed esterna) e allevia la tensione nella zona lombare.
fields.name: Shinbox IR stretch

fields.description:
• Purpose: Improves shoulder stability, posture, and scapular (shoulder blade) control. Often used in physical therapy and warm-ups.
  • Starting Position: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge forward at the hips (like a deadlift) keeping your back flat, or lie face down on an incline bench. Keep your thumbs pointing up throughout.
  • Y (Arms up): Extend both arms straight forward and upward at a 135-degree angle from your body, forming a "Y". Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
  • T (Arms out): Open your arms straight out to the sides, perpendicular to your body, forming a "T". Focus on pinching your shoulder blades.
  • W (Arms bent): Pull your elbows back, bending them to 90 degrees, and squeeze your shoulder blades to form a "W". Keep your wrists firm.
  • L (Arms rotated): Start with elbows bent at 90 degrees and close to your body. Rotate your forearms outward (external rotation) like a goalpost, forming an "L" or a gate.
  • Key Focus: Movements should be slow and controlled. Always lead with the thumbs and think about moving the shoulder blades, not just the arms.
fields.description_source:
* **Purpose:** Improves shoulder stability, posture, and scapular (shoulder blade) control. Often used in physical therapy and warm-ups.
* **Starting Position:**
  Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge forward at the hips (like a
  deadlift) keeping your back flat, or lie face down on an incline bench.
  Keep your thumbs pointing up throughout.
* **Y (Arms up):**
  Extend both arms straight forward and upward at a 135-degree angle from
  your body, forming a "Y". Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the
  top.
* **T (Arms out):** Open your arms straight out to the sides, perpendicular to your body, forming a "T". Focus on pinching your shoulder blades.
* **W (Arms bent):** Pull your elbows back, bending them to 90 degrees, and squeeze your shoulder blades to form a "W". Keep your wrists firm.
* **L (Arms rotated):**
  Start with elbows bent at 90 degrees and close to your body. Rotate
  your forearms outward (external rotation) like a goalpost, forming an
  "L" or a gate.
* **Key Focus:**
  Movements should be slow and controlled. Always lead with the thumbs
  and think about moving the shoulder blades, not just the arms.
fields.name: YTWL Exercise

fields.description:
• Obiettivo: Migliorare la stabilità della spalla, la postura e il controllo delle scapole. Ampiamente usato in fisioterapia e nel riscaldamento.
  • Posizione iniziale: In piedi con i piedi alla larghezza delle spalle. Piega il busto in avanti mantenendo la schiena dritta (come uno stacco), oppure sdraiati a pancia in giù su una panca inclinata. Tieni i pollici sempre rivolti verso l'alto.
  • Y (Braccia in alto): Distendi entrambe le braccia dritte in avanti e verso l'alto con un angolo di 135° rispetto al corpo, formando una "Y". Unisci le scapole nel punto più alto.
  • T (Braccia aperte): Apri le braccia lateralmente, perpendicolari al corpo, formando una "T". Concentrati sullo stringere le scapole.
  • W (Braccia piegate): Tira i gomiti indietro piegandoli a 90°, stringendo le scapole per formare una "W". Tieni i polsi fermi.
  • L (Rotazione esterna): Gomiti piegati a 90° e vicini al corpo. Ruota gli avambracci verso l'esterno (rotazione esterna) come dei pali della porta, formando una "L".
  • Attenzione: I movimenti devono essere lenti e controllati. Guida sempre il movimento con i pollici e concentrati sul movimento delle scapole, non solo delle braccia.
fields.description_source:
* **Obiettivo:**
  Migliorare la stabilità della spalla, la postura e il controllo delle
  scapole. Ampiamente usato in fisioterapia e nel riscaldamento.
* **Posizione iniziale:**
  In piedi con i piedi alla larghezza delle spalle. Piega il busto in
  avanti mantenendo la schiena dritta (come uno stacco), oppure sdraiati a
  pancia in giù su una panca inclinata. Tieni i pollici sempre rivolti
  verso l'alto.
* **Y (Braccia in alto):**
  Distendi entrambe le braccia dritte in avanti e verso l'alto con un
  angolo di 135° rispetto al corpo, formando una "Y". Unisci le scapole
  nel punto più alto.
* **T (Braccia aperte):** Apri le braccia lateralmente, perpendicolari al corpo, formando una "T". Concentrati sullo stringere le scapole.
* **W (Braccia piegate):** Tira i gomiti indietro piegandoli a 90°, stringendo le scapole per formare una "W". Tieni i polsi fermi.
* **L (Rotazione esterna):**
  Gomiti piegati a 90° e vicini al corpo. Ruota gli avambracci verso
  l'esterno (rotazione esterna) come dei pali della porta, formando una
  "L".
* **Attenzione:**
  I movimenti devono essere lenti e controllati. Guida sempre il
  movimento con i pollici e concentrati sul movimento delle scapole, non
  solo delle braccia.
fields.name: Esercizio YTWL

fields.description:
• Starting Position: Lie on your back (supine) on a mat, with your legs extended and relaxed. Arms can be placed alongside your body or out to the sides for stability.
  • The Movement: Slightly lift one leg off the floor (keeping it straight or with a slight bend). Move it laterally outward, away from the other leg, as if stepping over a small obstacle.
  • Range of Motion: Move the leg as far as comfortable without lifting your pelvis or rotating your torso, feeling a stretch in the inner thigh and hip.
  • Return: Slowly bring the leg back to the starting position with controlled movement.
  • Goal: Improves lateral hip mobility (abduction) and strengthens the pelvic stabilizer muscles.
fields.description_source:
* **Starting Position:**
  Lie on your back (supine) on a mat, with your legs extended and
  relaxed. Arms can be placed alongside your body or out to the sides for
  stability.
* **The Movement:**
  Slightly lift one leg off the floor (keeping it straight or with a
  slight bend). Move it laterally outward, away from the other leg, as if
  stepping over a small obstacle.
* **Range of Motion:**
  Move the leg as far as comfortable without lifting your pelvis or
  rotating your torso, feeling a stretch in the inner thigh and hip.
* **Return:** Slowly bring the leg back to the starting position with controlled movement.
* **Goal:** Improves lateral hip mobility (abduction) and strengthens the pelvic stabilizer muscles.
fields.name: Supine Hip Abduction

fields.description:
• Posizione iniziale: Sdraiati sulla schiena (supini) su un tappetino, con le gambe distese e rilassate. Le braccia possono essere lungo i fianchi o aperte per maggiore stabilità.
  • Il movimento: Solleva leggermente una gamba da terra (mantenendola tesa o con una leggera flessione). Spostala lateralmente verso l'esterno, allontanandola dall'altra gamba, come se dovessi scavalcare un piccolo ostacolo.
  • Ampiezza: Porta la gamba più in là possibile senza sollevare il bacino o ruotare il busto, sentendo un allungamento nella parte interna della coscia e dell'anca.
  • Ritorno: Riporta lentamente la gamba alla posizione iniziale, controllando il movimento.
  • Obiettivo: Migliora la mobilità laterale dell'anca (abduzione) e allena i muscoli stabilizzatori del bacino.

fields.name: Abduzione dell'anca in posizione supina

fields.description:
• Setup: Lie on a decline bench with your head at the top and grip the handles.
  • The Start: Extend legs fully, keeping them slightly elevated to engage your core.
  • The Lift: Raise your legs toward the ceiling using your abs, not momentum.
  • The Squeeze: Pause and contract your lower abdominals at the top.
  • The Return: Lower your legs slowly to the starting position with control.
fields.description_source:
- **Setup**: Lie on a decline bench with your head at the top and grip the handles.
- **The Start**: Extend legs fully, keeping them slightly elevated to engage your core.
- **The Lift**: Raise your legs toward the ceiling using your abs, not momentum.
- **The Squeeze**: Pause and contract your lower abdominals at the top.
- **The Return**: Lower your legs slowly to the starting position with control.
fields.name: Decline Bench Leg Raise

fields.description:
• Setup: Stand with feet together and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders.
  • Position: Rest the bar on your upper chest.
  • Press: Brace your core and push the bar straight up until your arms lock out.
  • Finish: Control the bar back down to your chest.
  • https://youtu.be/ZXpdJOLNoWw?si=u27cGyODoblXHBu1
fields.description_source:
- **Setup:** Stand with feet together and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders.
- **Position:** Rest the bar on your upper chest.
- **Press:** Brace your core and push the bar straight up until your arms lock out.
- **Finish:** Control the bar back down to your chest.
- https://youtu.be/ZXpdJOLNoWw?si=u27cGyODoblXHBu1
fields.name: Overhead Barbell Press

fields.description:
• Setup: Lie flat on the bench and grip the bar at shoulder-width (closer than a standard bench press).
  • Descent: Lower the bar with control to your lower chest.
  • Form: Keep your elbows tucked in tight against your torso—do not let them flare out.
  • Ascent: Press the bar straight back up, squeezing your triceps at the top.
fields.description_source:
- **Setup:** Lie flat on the bench and grip the bar at shoulder-width (closer than a standard bench press).
- **Descent:** Lower the bar with control to your lower chest.
- **Form:** Keep your elbows tucked in tight against your torso—do not let them flare out.
- **Ascent:** Press the bar straight back up, squeezing your triceps at the top.
fields.name: Close-Grip Bench Press

fields.description:
• Setup: Stand facing the cable, feet shoulder-width apart, with a slight forward lean.
  • Grip: Hold the rope with palms facing each other and elbows tucked tight to your ribs.
  • Execution: Push the rope down until arms are fully straight, pulling the ends apart at the bottom.
  • Control: Keep upper arms still and return to the start with a slow, controlled motion.
fields.description_source:
- **Setup:** Stand facing the cable, feet shoulder-width apart, with a slight forward lean.
- **Grip:** Hold the rope with palms facing each other and elbows tucked tight to your ribs.
- **Execution:** Push the rope down until arms are fully straight, pulling the ends apart at the bottom.
- **Control:** Keep upper arms still and return to the start with a slow, controlled motion.
fields.name: Tricep Rope Pushdowns

fields.description:
• The First Pull: Lift the bar from the floor to knee-height by driving with the legs, keeping the angle of your back constant.
  • The "Power" Position: As the bar reaches mid-thigh, explosively shrug and extend your hips to create vertical momentum.
  • The Quick Elbow Turnover: In the catch phase, rotate your elbows rapidly under the bar to create a "shelf" on your front deltoids.
  • The Vertical Press: Press the bar in a straight line; your head should shift slightly forward once the bar clears your forehead to reach full lockout.
fields.description_source:
* **The First Pull:** Lift the bar from the floor to knee-height by driving with the legs, keeping the angle of your back constant.
* **The "Power" Position:** As the bar reaches mid-thigh, explosively shrug and extend your hips to create vertical momentum.
* **The Quick Elbow Turnover:** In the catch phase, rotate your elbows rapidly under the bar to create a "shelf" on your front deltoids.
* **The Vertical Press:** Press the bar in a straight line; your head should shift slightly forward once the bar clears your forehead to reach full lockout.
fields.name: Clean and Press

fields.description:
• Setup: Begin in a standard plank position with a weight plate balanced securely on your upper back.
  • Alignment: Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels, engaging your core to prevent your back from sagging.
  • Execution: Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, keeping your elbows at a forty-five degrees.
  • Ascent: Press through your palms to return to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
fields.description_source:
- **Setup:** Begin in a standard plank position with a weight plate balanced securely on your upper back.
- **Alignment:** Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels, engaging your core to prevent your back from sagging.
- **Execution:** Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor, keeping your elbows at a **forty-five degrees**.
- **Ascent:** Press through your palms to return to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
fields.name: Weighted push-ups

fields.description:
• Starting Position: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands either on your hips or holding dumbbells at your sides.
  • The Stride: Take a wide step forward with your right leg.
  • The Descent: Lower your hips until both knees are bent at approximately 90-degree angles.
  • The Transition: Drive through your front heel to stand up, bringing your back foot forward to step directly into the next lunge.
fields.description_source:
- **Starting Position:** Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands either on your hips or holding dumbbells at your sides.
- **The Stride:** Take a wide step forward with your right leg.
- **The Descent:** Lower your hips until both knees are bent at approximately **90-degree angles**.
- **The Transition:** Drive through your front heel to stand up, bringing your back foot forward to step directly into the next lunge.
fields.name: Walking Lunges

Lay on the back and lift legs in perpendicular fashion, keeping the knees together.

Start making small circles in one direction with both of your knees with very small range of motion, focusing on abs and keeping your back in contact with the floor.

fields.description:
Distribute your body weight evenly on all knees and arms. Keep the torso stable, with the abdomen engaged and the lumbar spine straight without twisting the back (imagine: a glass on your lower back won't fall off). Raise one leg outwards without twisting it.

Pay attention to the right angle at the hips and knees. Always train both sides of the body.

To increase resistance, a resistance band can be used.

fields.description:
1. Place a bench between two cable pulleys and adjust the pulleys so they are at chest/shoulder height when you are seated.
  2. Grab the handles with a neutral grip and lean against the upright backrest.
  3. Bring your arms together in front of your chest in a controlled motion, focusing on squeezing your chest muscles. Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement.
  4. Pause briefly at the top position.
  5. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position while maintaining control of the movement.
  6. Repeat for reps.

fields.description: Hampelmänner sind eine plyometrische Übung. Plyometrische Übungen sind explosive, aerobe Bewegungen, die Geschwindigkeit, Schnelligkeit und Kraft steigern und den ganzen Körper trainieren. Hampelmänner beanspruchen die Arme, Schultern, Bauchmuskeln, Hüftmuskeln und Hüftbeuger sowie die Oberschenkel, Kniesehnen und den vierköpfigen Oberschenkelmuskel (Quadrizeps). Sprünge sind gesundheitsfördernd, da sie Krafttraining mit Herz-Kreislauf-Training kombinieren. Da Sprünge den Puls erhöhen, können sie auch die kardiovaskuläre Fitness verbessern.

fields.description:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding the barbell with both hands in front just past shoulder width.
  2. Bend forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, keeping your back straight.
  3. Engage your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to touch them together.
  4. Release the shrug. Description taken from MuscleWiki
fields.description_source:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding the barbell with both hands in front just past shoulder width.
2. Bend forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, keeping your back straight.
3. Engage your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to touch them together.
4. Release the shrug.
Description taken from MuscleWiki
fields.name: Barbell Silverback Shrug

fields.description: The Inverted Lat Pull Down (most commonly known as the Reverse Grip Lat Pull Down) is a variation of the standard exercise that uses an underhand (supinated) grip with hands at shoulder-width. This position shifts more emphasis to the lower lats and increases biceps involvement, often allowing for a greater range of motion and a deeper squeeze at the bottom of the movement.
fields.description_source: The **Inverted Lat Pull Down** (most commonly known as the **Reverse Grip Lat Pull Down**) is a variation of the standard exercise that uses an underhand (supinated) grip with hands at shoulder-width. This position shifts more emphasis to the **lower lats** and increases **biceps** involvement, often allowing for a greater range of motion and a deeper squeeze at the bottom of the movement.
fields.name: Inverted Lat Pull Down

fields.description: The Seated V-Grip Row is a compound pulling exercise performed on a cable machine using a close-grip "V" handle. While seated with your feet braced, you pull the handle toward your midsection, focusing on back thickness and mid-back development.
fields.description_source: The **Seated V-Grip Row** is a compound pulling exercise performed on a cable machine using a close-grip "V" handle. While seated with your feet braced, you pull the handle toward your midsection, focusing on **back thickness** and mid-back development.
fields.name: Seated V-Grip Row

fields.description: The Assisted Pull-Up is a compound vertical pulling exercise performed on a machine with a counterweight platform or by using resistance bands. It mimics the mechanics of a standard pull-up but reduces the total weight you have to lift, making it an excellent tool for building functional strength and perfecting your technique.
fields.description_source: The **Assisted Pull-Up** is a compound vertical pulling exercise performed on a machine with a counterweight platform or by using resistance bands. It mimics the mechanics of a standard pull-up but reduces the total weight you have to lift, making it an excellent tool for building functional strength and perfecting your technique.
fields.name: Assisted Pull-Up

fields.description: The Dumbbell Curl is a classic isolation exercise for the arms. Unlike the Hammer Curl, it involves rotating your wrists (supination) as you lift the weights, which allows for a full contraction of the biceps.
fields.description_source: The **Dumbbell Curl** is a classic isolation exercise for the arms. Unlike the Hammer Curl, it involves rotating your wrists (supination) as you lift the weights, which allows for a full contraction of the biceps.
fields.name: Dumbbell Curl

fields.description: The Hammer Curl is a popular isolation exercise for the arms, typically performed with dumbbells. Instead of rotating your wrists like a standard bicep curl, you keep a neutral grip (palms facing each other) throughout the entire movement, as if you were holding a hammer.
fields.description_source:

dumbbells. Instead of rotating your wrists like a standard bicep curl,

fields.description: The Abdominal Crunch is a classic core isolation exercise performed on the floor or a mat. Unlike a full sit-up, it involves a smaller range of motion where you only lift your shoulders and upper back off the ground, keeping your lower back pressed firmly against the floor.

fields.name: Abdominal Crunch

fields.description: Belt Squat: a squat variation done with a belt attached to a loading machine or platform so the weight is supported at the hips instead of on the spine. It mainly trains the quads and glutes, with much less lower-back fatigue than a barbell squat. Use a full range of motion, keep the torso upright, and drive through the mid-foot.

fields.description: Cable rear delt fly (single arm): Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set around shoulder height. With a slight bend in the elbow, pull the handle out and back in a wide arc so the upper arm moves away from the body. Keep your torso still and focus on the rear delt doing the work. Use light to moderate weight and controlled reps.
fields.description_source:
**Cable rear delt fly (single arm):**  
Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set around shoulder height. With a slight bend in the elbow, pull the handle out and back in a wide arc so the upper arm moves away from the body. Keep your torso still and focus on the rear delt doing the work. Use **light to moderate weight** and controlled reps.
fields.name: Cable Rear-Delt Fly (single arm)

fields.description:
Bewegungsablauf wie beim normalen vorgebeugtem Rudern, nur die Handstellung ist reverse, also die Handfläche zeigen nach hinten.

Greife die Langhantel mit breitem (etwas mehr als Schulterbreit) Griff und beuge dich nach vorne. Dabei ist der Oberkörper nicht ganz Waagerecht, sonder ein bisschen steiler, der Kopf schaut nach vorn und die Brust is rausgestreckt.

Ziehe nun das Gewicht in richtung Bauchnabel. Schwinge während der Bewegung den Oberkörper nicht und halte die Arme dicht am Körper wenn du sie hochziehst. Bringe die Hantel langsam wieder nach unten

Lift the bar back to the starting position by extending the elbow and exhaling.
